## PAGE 00271: (2026x) Adjunct property

- page_id: `255626340`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626340/2026x+Adjunct+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:39.940+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['adjunct-property']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition **principal** **: Element[1]** is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter.

If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**•** CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction. 
**•** ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.

The preceding shows an adjunct property in the **Integrator** activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity **TimeDifferent**. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block **Integrator** also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Classifier Behavior property' space=''] for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block **Integrator**, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action **getTimeDiff**, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the **getTimeDiff** and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the **Variables** pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition <strong>principal</strong> <strong>: Element[1]</strong> is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter. </p><p>If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee7410-4acb-4e4c-8765-ac3cb789e79f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction.<br /><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties" ac:alt="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties"><ri:attachment ri:filename="execute_objectwithAdjunct.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adjunct property" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.</h6><p>The preceding shows an adjunct property in the <strong>Integrator</strong> activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity <strong>TimeDifferent</strong>. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block <strong>Integrator</strong> also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Classifier Behavior property" /></ac:link> for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block <strong>Integrator</strong>, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action <strong>getTimeDiff</strong>, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the <strong>getTimeDiff</strong> and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane. </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625275 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00273: (2026x) Animation

- page_id: `255625275`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625275/2026x+Animation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:40.954+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['simulation-animation', 'animation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1440884490

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1440884492

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1440884491

Active elements on a diagram will be annotated during simulation using the same annotation mechanism used in the active validation:

- Active and visited elements will be annotated in red and green respectively.
- Runtime values will be visible in the tooltip of annotated elements.
- Values flown through pins of action will be visible in the tooltip of annotated actions.
- Duration of called Activities will be visible in the tooltip of annotated call Behavior Actions if you simulate the Activity with a specific duration mode.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The following tags in the SImulation Configuration help you with the animations:

- silent - If*true*, the simulation will run without animation or idle time.
- **showAnnotations** - If *true,* the simulation annotations in the diagrams are enabled. If**showAnnotations** is set to*false*, then a few animation features are affected.

For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling' space=''].

1440884489

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626093 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00274: (2026x) Animation speed option

- page_id: `255626093`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626093/2026x+Animation+speed+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:31.620+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Animation
- labels: ['animation-speed']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Animation Speed** in the Simulation Configuration is the speed to run a particular Configuration animation. The default value of Animation Speed is **95%**.****You can adjust it to avoid problems with delay when the default speed is slow and large numbers of model elements are running. To run your animation at a pace that is faster or slower than the default animation speed, change the value of the **Animation** **Speed** option to your preferred one in the Specification window of the *<<SimulationConfig>>*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Animation Speed option in <<SimulationConfig>> is specified at the default value of 95%.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**Animation Speed** will not affect animation in the Parametric diagram since no animation is delayed during parametric solving.

The **Animation Speed** setting is visible through diagram animation. For example, if the speed is specified at the value of 50%, the animation must be slower than the default value 95%. The speed setting is consistent with the Simulation speed bar control; e.g., if the animation speed is specified at 50%, the speed bar is set to the middle as shown in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The animation speed selected is consistent with the Animation speed bar.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The speed of the animation does not affect the animation in the Parametric diagram as no animation is delayed during parametric solving.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The default value of Animation Speed is <strong>95%</strong>.<strong> </strong>You can adjust it to avoid problems with delay when the default speed is slow and large numbers of model elements are running. To run your animation at a pace that is faster or slower than the default animation speed, change the value of the <strong>Animation</strong> <strong>Speed</strong> option to your preferred one in the Specification window of the <em>&lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt;</em>.</p><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The default animation speed in the Simulation Config is 95." ac:alt="The default animation speed in the Simulation Config is 95."><ri:attachment ri:filename="animation_speed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Animation speed option" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Animation Speed option in &lt;&lt;SimulationConfig&gt;&gt; is specified at the default value of 95%.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba65e88c-9124-4081-b7fa-c5732540244c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Animation Speed</strong> will not affect animation in the Parametric diagram since no animation is delayed during parametric solving.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="Textbody">The <strong>Animation Speed</strong> setting is visible through diagram animation. For example, if the speed is specified at the value of 50%, the animation must be slower than the default value 95%. The speed setting is consistent with the Simulation speed bar control; e.g., if the animation speed is specified at 50%, the speed bar is set to the middle as shown in the following figure </p><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The animation speed is consistent with the animation speed bar." ac:alt="The animation speed is consistent with the animation speed bar."><ri:attachment ri:filename="anim_speedbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Animation speed option" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The animation speed selected is consistent with the Animation speed bar.</h6><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69901d8a-8dae-4595-a42b-df8d70ed1eb4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The speed of the animation does not affect the animation in the Parametric diagram as no animation is delayed during parametric solving.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624756 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00275: (2026x) Association Block

- page_id: `255624756`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624756/2026x+Association+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:00.764+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['association-block']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Block stereotype extends Class so it can be applied to any specialization of Class including Association Classes. Association Classes are informally called “Association Blocks.” An Association Block can own properties and connectors just like any other Block. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end Classifiers of the Association. To refer to the linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which (participant) properties of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the Association. The value of a participant property on the instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the Association.

The following figures depict the use of an Association Block in a model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### System initializes «ConnectorProperty» by an Association Block instance according to "Connector" reference (AB) and «ParticipantProperty» (b2, b1. add. b21. b11) according to "End" reference (b1, b2).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Association Block can provide or add anything such as Behaviors and connectors (a, b) so signals can flow. Parametric and all other constraints (a = b + 5) work automatically.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Standard">A Block stereotype extends Class so it can be applied to any specialization of Class including Association Classes. Association Classes are informally called “Association Blocks.” An Association Block can own properties and connectors just like any other Block. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end Classifiers of the Association. To refer to the linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which (participant) properties of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the Association. The value of a participant property on the instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the Association.</p><p class="Standard">The following figures depict the use of an Association Block in a model.</p><p class="Standard"><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connectorproperties.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Association Block" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">System initializes <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8de74124-7f84-4779-963e-c038f38c8909">«ConnectorProperty»</ac:inline-comment-marker> by an Association Block instance according to &quot;Connector&quot; reference (AB) and «ParticipantProperty» (b2, b1. add. b21. b11) according to &quot;End&quot; reference (b1, b2).</h6><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connectors.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Association Block" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">An Association Block can provide or add anything such as Behaviors and connectors (a, b) so signals can flow. Parametric and all other constraints (a = b + 5) work automatically.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626010 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00276: (2026x) Attached files supported

- page_id: `255626010`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626010/2026x+Attached+files+supported
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:22.037+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['attached-file-support']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

610531277

610531279

610531278

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can access files as «AttachedFile» in MagicDraw using only file names (without paths), if there are references to files by names not found in project directories. The attached files supported are as follows:

- **CSV**: Used by the **LoadCSVFile** tag of «TimeSeriesChart». [IMAGE alt='' src='']An attached file of BouncingResult.csv used by the LoadCSVFile tag of «TimeSeriesChart».
- XLSX (Excel) : Used by Excel Helper APIs.
- M (MATLAB) : Used by the Specification tag of «Constraint».
- FMU (FMI) : Used as a «Block» «fmu» for FMI.
- JAR (external libraries) : Used as external libraries (*.JAR) in the script engine.
- SLX : used by the «url» tag of «SimulinkBlock»
- MOS/MO : used by Dymola.

However, if the referenced file is in both the project directory and in the attached file, the following conditions are applied:

1. If the file is in both the project directory and the attached file, the file in «AttachedFile» will be used, and the following warning message will appear in the Console pane, " Warning: Use <file name>in the attached file instead of the project directory. "
2. If the file is in the project directory but not in the attached file, the file in the project directory will be used without any displayed messages.
3. If the file is not in the project directory but is in the attached file, the file in «AttachedFile» will be used, and the following information message will appear in the Console pane, “ INFO: The attached file <file name> is loaded. ”
4. If the file is not in the project directory and not in the attached file, the following error message will appear in the Console pane, “ ERROR: Cannot find file or attached file with '<file name>'.
5. If the mos/mo file (Dymola) is located in both the working directory and the attached file, the file in the current working directory will be used, and the following warning message will appear in the Console pane, "Warning: Use '<file name>.ext' in the working directory instead of the attached file."

610531276

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Projects with file attachments' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel Import Plugin Documentation' space='EI']

ri:space-key="EI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel import plugin documentation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625881 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00277: (2026x) Auto-generating HTML files

- page_id: `255625881`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625881/2026x+Auto-generating+HTML+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:12.268+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > (2026x) HTML UI
- labels: ['generating-html-files', 'html-files']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

33

If the **Show UI As HTML**property of a Simulation Configuration is set to*true,*the Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically generate HTML files from all used UI Configurations. You can use HTML UI Mockups instead of local UI Mockups and control your model simulation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a Simulation Configuration with the Show UI As HTML property set to true to auto-generate HTML files.

When the Simulation Configurationis run, the embedded browser will open, HTML files with a hyperlink to each file in the local project folder will be generated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulation Console pane.

##### Embedded browser

By default, the HTML UI opens once you run the simulation configuration, where you can view and control the simulation. The Embedded Browser allows you to show any web pages in dock-able embedded windows in fixed places. These windows can be moved and docked, and will appear in themodeling toolwindow. When you open a saved project or re-run the project with the embedded browser option, those windows will automatically be shown in the same locations, States (docked/floating), and sizes. When the simulation is terminated, these windows will be closed automatically.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Embedded browser UI with Control Panel in modeling tool.

##### Generating HTML files

Depending on the circumstances, you may want to manually generate HTML files for further customization of the UI Mockups. You can manually generate HTML files through either the UI diagram or HTML supported controls using the **Generate HTML**command. However, if the files already exist, a warning message box will open to prevent you from accidentally overwriting them. All generated HTML files will be written and stored in the project's working directory.

To generate HTML

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the User Interface Modeling Diagram toolbar, click **Generate HTML**. HTML files are generated for all the UI elements shown in the diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the User Interface Modeling diagram or UI element and select Simulation>Generate HTML. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Internal Block Definition Diagram, right-click the Widget property and select Simulation > Generate HTML. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. If you get a warning that the file already exists, click Yes to replace the existing file, or click No to cancel the command. After completing the steps above, you should get a notification that the files were generated successfully.

##### Generating and attaching HTML files to Teamwork Cloud projects

You can easily generate and attach HTML files through the UI Modeling diagram (for only the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Server-side simulation' space='']) when the project is hosted on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Teamwork Cloud and Services' space='MCS'].

To generate and attach HTML

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the User Interface Modeling Diagram toolbar, click **Generate and Attach HTML**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the User Interface Modeling diagram or UI element and select Simulation>Generate HTML. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate and Attach HTML_Conatainment tree.png']
2. If you get a warning that the file already exists, click**Yes**to replace the existing file, or click**No**to cancel the command.After completing the steps above, you should get a notification that the files were generated successfully.

Every Frame/Table element will automatically generate HTML files that are zipped and attached under the element with the same name as the UI element name. You should get a notification in the**Console**tab with the list of all the files attached to the project.The structure of the zipped file does not include reusable files of the Simulation, for example “js” folder, and “css” files.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Simulation console' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626294 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00278: (2026x) Automatic and manual initialization of objects/values

- page_id: `255626294`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626294/2026x+Automatic+and+manual+initialization+of+objects+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:37.582+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator
- labels: ['initializing-objects', 'initializing-values']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Solve After Initialization** option allows modelers to control initialization of objects and values automatically or manually from the initial parametric constraints solving. This flexibility improves the parametric debugging experience. You can select the option through the Specification window of the Simulation Config, or by clicking **Options** >**Project** > **Simulation** > **Solve After Initialization** as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Solve After Initialization option can initialize objects and values automatically.

The value of the **Solve After Initialization** option is **true** by default to enable automatic initial solving. If you wish to manually invoke initial solving, you can do so by clearing the check box (the value will change to **false**), clicking **Refresh** in the **Variables** pane, or pressing **Start** (**F8**).

To invoke initial parametric solving manually:

1. Open/create a project with a parametric diagram.
2. In the Simulation Config, set the “Solve After Initialization” and “Auto Start” option to **false** (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Run simulation via the Simulation Config. All related results will not be calculated automatically, so you need to manually invoke it via the Refresh in Variables pane or Start (F8) (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='3.PNG']
4. Select true for the Solve After Initialization option, and run the Simulation Config. All related results will be calculated automatically (see the following figure) [ATTACHMENT filename='4.PNG']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Solve After Initialization</strong> option allows modelers to control initialization of objects and values automatically or manually from the initial parametric constraints solving. This flexibility improves the parametric debugging experience. You can select the option through the Specification window of the Simulation Config, or by clicking <strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Project</strong> &gt; <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Solve After Initialization</strong> as shown in the following figure.</p><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Automatic or manually initializing objects using the Solve After Initialization option" ac:alt="Automatic or manually initializing objects using the Solve After Initialization option"><ri:attachment ri:filename="1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic and manual initialization of objects/values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">The Solve After Initialization option can initialize objects and values automatically.</h6><p class="Standard"> </p><p class="Standard">The value of the <strong>Solve After Initialization</strong> option is <strong>true</strong> by default to enable automatic initial solving. If you wish to manually invoke initial solving, you can do so by clearing the check box (the value will change to <strong>false</strong>), clicking <strong>Refresh</strong> in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane, or pressing <strong>Start</strong> (<strong>F8</strong>).</p><p class="Standard"> </p><p>To invoke initial parametric solving manually:</p><hr /><ol><li>Open/create a project with a parametric diagram.</li><li><p>In the Simulation Config, set the “Solve After Initialization” and “Auto Start” option to <strong>false</strong> (see the following figure).</p><h6><ac:image ac:title="Selecting false to manually invoke objects or values." ac:alt="Selecting false to manually invoke objects or values."><ri:attachment ri:filename="2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic and manual initialization of objects/values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li>Run simulation via the Simulation Config. All related results will not be calculated automatically, so you need to manually invoke it via the Refresh in Variables pane or Start (F8) (see the following figure).<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic and manual initialization of objects/values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></li><li>Select <strong>true</strong> for the <strong>Solve After Initialization</strong> option, and run the Simulation Config. All related results will be calculated automatically (see the following figure)<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Auto initialization of objects/value calculate results automatically."><ri:attachment ri:filename="4.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic and manual initialization of objects/values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625454 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00279: (2026x) Automatic initialization of context and runtime objects

- page_id: `255625454`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625454/2026x+Automatic+initialization+of+context+and+runtime+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:45.937+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation debugging > (2026x) Runtime Value Monitoring
- labels: ['context-initialization', 'runtime-object-initialization']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

41098601

41098603

41098600

**On this page**

22

41098602

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is capable of initializing both context and runtime objects automatically even if a simulation context does not exist, and no object is configured to initiate the runtime objects.

#### Context initialization

When executing a Behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to simulate its context as well.

To simulate a Behavior with its context

- Right-click a Behavior or a Behavior's diagram and select Simulation > Run with Context to execute it together with its context.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To simulate a Behavior without its context

- Right-click a Behavior or a Behavior's diagram and select Simulation > Run to execute it without its context.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When you simulate a Behavior with its context, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the context of the selected Behavior and simulate the Classifier Behavior. During the Classifier Behavior simulation, if the runtime object of the context Classifier is stable, the selected Behavior will be simulated.

If a selected Behavior has no context, the **Run** **with** **Context** menu will be disabled.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Running a model simulation using the shortcut menu.

#### Runtime object initialization

When you use an **InstanceSpecification** to initialize a runtime object, you will also need to use the corresponding slot values of such **InstanceSpecification** to initialize the feature values of the runtime object. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the default value of the corresponding feature (property) of each slot if the slot value is empty.

The value of each feature (property) will be automatically initialized only if its **Lower-value** **Multiplicity** is more than zero or is undefined. Otherwise (the feature's **Lower-value** **Multiplicity** is zero), the feature value will be empty (nothing is initialized). However, characteristic of the initialization depends on the type of the feature. The following sections explain the types of feature that can influence initialization characteristics

##### Structural feature typed by primitive datatype

The default value of a structural feature, which is typed by a primitive datatype, is its initialized value.This default initialization value, which can be set from **Options** **>** **Project** **>****Simulation** as shown in following figure. When true and value property has no default value, the value will be initialized to 0 value. The default value is **false**.

The datatypes which are affected by this option are listed in the table as follows

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Project Options - Initialize Empty Values to 0.

This initialization value applies to numerical datatypes only. For other types, see the table for values used for automatic initialization of the Structural Feature typed by primitive datatype as follows.**

| Primitive DataType (QualifiedName) | Value |
| --- | --- |
| UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::BooleanPrimitiveValueTypes::Boolean (SysML Profile.mdzip)UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::boolean | false |
| UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::IntegerUML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::UnlimitedNaturalPrimitiveValueTypes::Integer (SysML Profile.mdzip)PrimitiveValueTypes::UnlimitedNatural (SysML Profile.mdzip)UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::intUML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::longUML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::short | All numerical data types pick up their default initialization value from the Project Options as shown in the above figure. |
| UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::RealPrimitiveValueTypes::Real (SysML Profile.mdzip)UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::doubleUML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::float | All numerical data types pick up their default initialization value from the Project Options as shown in the above figure. |
| Enumeration | • UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::Real |
| UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::StringPrimitiveValueTypes::String (SysML Profile.mdzip) | "" (Empty String) |

##### Structural feature typed by non-datatype

If the type of a structural feature is not a datatype, the conditions are applied as follows

- If the structural feature has a non-empty slot value, it will be initialized with such value.
- Otherwise, if the structural feature's default value is specified, it will be initialized with the default value.

If the structural feature also has internal feature(s), for each internal feature, the conditions are applied as follows

- If the internal feature has a non-empty slot value, it will be initialized with such value.
- Otherwise, if the internal feature's default value is specified, it will be initialized with the default value.

If an internal feature also has its own internal feature(s), the same rule above will be applied recursively.

##### Structural feature typed by user-defined eatatype

For a structural feature typed by a user-defined datatype, the conditions are applied as follows

- If the Datatype has internal structural feature(s), the value of the structural feature will be initialized as described in .
- If the Datatype has no internal structural feature, the following conditions are applied
  - The value of the structural feature will be initialized as described in if the Datatype is inherited from a primitive datatype.
  - The initialized value of the structural feature will be its default value if the Datatype is not inherited from any primitive datatype. If the default value of the feature is, however, not specified, the initialized value will be empty.

42669394

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Classifier Behavior property' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Manipulating structural values' space='']

ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic initialization of context and runtime objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>When you simulate a Behavior with its context, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the context of the selected Behavior and simulate the Classifier Behavior. During the Classifier Behavior simulation, if the runtime object of the context Classifier is stable, the selected Behavior will be simulated. </p><p>If a selected Behavior has no context, the <strong>Run</strong> <strong>with</strong> <strong>Context</strong> menu will be disabled.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SIM-2085_newFig3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic initialization of context and runtime objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Running a model simulation using the shortcut menu.</h6><h2>Runtime object initialization </h2><p>When you use an <strong>InstanceSpecification</strong> to initialize a runtime object, you will also need to use the corresponding slot values of such <strong>InstanceSpecification</strong> to initialize the feature values of the runtime object. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the default value of the corresponding feature (property) of each slot if the slot value is empty. </p><p>The value of each feature (property) will be automatically initialized only if its <strong>Lower-value</strong> <strong>Multiplicity</strong> is more than zero or is undefined. Otherwise (the feature's <strong>Lower-value</strong> <strong>Multiplicity</strong> is zero), the feature value will be empty (nothing is initialized). However, characteristic of the initialization depends on the type of the feature. The following sections explain the types of feature that can influence initialization characteristics</p><h3>Structural feature typed by primitive datatype </h3><p>The default value of a structural feature, which is typed by a primitive datatype, is its initialized value.This default initialization value, which can be set from <strong>Options</strong> <strong>&gt;</strong> <strong>Project</strong> <strong>&gt; </strong><strong>Simulation</strong> as shown in following figure. When true and value property has no default value, the value will be initialized to 0 value. The default value is <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">false</strong>.</p><p>The datatypes which are affected by this option are listed in the table as follows</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="initialize-numerical-value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic initialization of context and runtime objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Simulation Project Options - Initialize Empty Values to 0.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">This initialization value applies to numerical datatypes only. For other types, see the table for values used for automatic initialization of the Structural Feature typed by primitive datatype as follows.<em><br /></em></p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 89.5072%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 45.6889%;" /><col style="width: 54.3111%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><strong>Primitive DataType (Qualified</strong><strong>Name)</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr><tr><td><ul><li>UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>Boolean</strong></li><li>PrimitiveValueTypes::<strong>Boolean</strong> (SysML Profile.mdzip)</li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>boolean</strong></li></ul></td><td>false</td></tr><tr><td><ul><li>UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>Integer</strong></li><li>UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>UnlimitedNatural</strong></li><li>PrimitiveValueTypes::<strong>Integer</strong> (SysML Profile.mdzip)</li><li>PrimitiveValueTypes::<strong>UnlimitedNatural</strong> (SysML Profile.mdzip)</li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>int</strong></li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>long</strong></li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>short</strong></li></ul></td><td>All numerical data types pick up their default initialization value from the Project Options as shown in the above figure.</td></tr><tr><td><ul><li>UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>Real</strong></li><li>PrimitiveValueTypes::<strong>Real</strong> (SysML Profile.mdzip)</li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>double</strong></li><li>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile::datatypes::<strong>float</strong></li></ul></td><td>All numerical data types pick up their default initialization value from the Project Options as shown in the above figure.</td></tr><tr><td><ul><li><strong>Enumeration</strong></li></ul></td><td><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>Real</strong></td></tr><tr><td><ul><li>UML Standard Profile::UML2 Metamodel::PrimitiveTypes::<strong>String</strong></li><li>PrimitiveValueTypes::<strong>String</strong> (SysML Profile.mdzip)</li></ul></td><td>&quot;&quot; (Empty String)</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Structural feature typed by non-datatype</h3><p>If the type of a structural feature is not a datatype, the conditions are applied as follows</p><ul><li>If the structural feature has a non-empty slot value, it will be initialized with such value. </li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Otherwise, if the structural feature's default value is specified, it will be initialized with the default value. </span></li></ul><p>If the structural feature also has internal feature(s), for each internal feature, the conditions are applied as follows</p><ul><li>If the internal feature has a non-empty slot value, it will be initialized with such value. </li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Otherwise, if the internal feature's default value is specified, it will be initialized with the default value. </span></li></ul><p>If an internal feature also has its own internal feature(s), the same rule above will be applied recursively. </p><h3>Structural feature typed by user-defined eatatype</h3><p>For a structural feature typed by a user-defined datatype, the conditions are applied as follows</p><ul><li>If the Datatype has internal structural feature(s), the value of the structural feature will be initialized as described in <ac:link ac:anchor="StructuralfeatureTypedbyNon-Datatype"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Structural feature typed by non-datatype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">If the Datatype has no internal structural feature, the following conditions are applied <br /></span><ul><li>The value of the structural feature will be initialized as described in <ac:link ac:anchor="Structuralfeaturetypedbyprimitivedatatype"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Structural feature typed by primitive datatype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> if the Datatype is inherited from a primitive datatype.</li><li>The initialized value of the structural feature will be its default value if the Datatype is not inherited from any primitive datatype. If the default value of the feature is, however, not specified, the initialized value will be empty. </li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0975e1f2-410a-4d72-a793-fe6f19405461"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">42669394</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Classifier Behavior property" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Getting a structural feature value"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Manipulating structural values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Getting a structural feature value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625852 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00280: (2026x) Automatic start of active objects

- page_id: `255625852`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625852/2026x+Automatic+start+of+active+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:09.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['automatic-start-of-active-objects', 'active-objects']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An active object is an object whose Classifier is an active Class, a Class element with isActive = true. When you run a Simulation Configuration with **autoStartActiveObjects** = true, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will start the Classifier Behavior of the active Class right after the object is instantiated. The following figure shows the stereo system's Simulation Configuration where the value of **autoStartActiveObjects** is true. In this example, the Behavior of the active objects (Speaker, Headphone, and Player) will start automatically once the object starts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Simulation Configuration.

If the value of **autoStartActiveObjects** is false, or if the Classes or blocks are not active Classes, you have to start each object using a **startObjectBehavior** Action. The first figure below shows the **System init** Activity, which is the Classifier Behavior of a stereo system block. This Behavior uses the **startObjectBehavior** Actions to start the Behaviors of objects, which are the internal parts of the stereo system object (big speakers, small speakers, and dvd player). You can simplify the **System init** activity, as shown in the second figure below, by making the blocks typing the big speakers, small speakers, and dvd player, to be active blocks then you can simulate the stereo system through a Simulation Configuration with autoStartActiveObjects = true.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### System init Activity to start the Behavior of runtime objects using startObjectBehavior Actions. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Simplified System init Activity to be used with autoStartActiveObjects whose value is true.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An active object is an object whose Classifier is an active Class, a Class element with isActive = true. When you run a Simulation Configuration with <strong>autoStartActiveObjects</strong> = true, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will start the Classifier Behavior of the active Class right after the object is instantiated. The following figure shows the stereo system's Simulation Configuration where the value of <strong>autoStartActiveObjects</strong> is true. In this example, the Behavior of the active objects (Speaker, Headphone, and Player) will start automatically once the object starts. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AutoStartActiveObjects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic start of active objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Simulation Configuration.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">If the value of <strong>autoStartActiveObjects</strong> is false, or if the Classes or blocks are not active Classes, you have to start each object using a <strong>startObjectBehavior</strong> Action. The first figure below shows the <strong>System init</strong> Activity, which is the Classifier Behavior of a stereo system block. This Behavior uses the <strong>startObjectBehavior</strong> Actions to start the Behaviors of objects, which are the internal parts of the stereo system object (big speakers, small speakers, and dvd player). You can simplify the <strong>System init</strong> activity, as shown in the second figure below, by making the blocks typing the big speakers, small speakers, and dvd player, to be active blocks then you can simulate the stereo system through a Simulation Configuration with autoStartActiveObjects = true. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="System init Activity to Start the Behavior of Runtime Objects Using startObjectBehavior Actions" ac:alt="System init Activity to Start the Behavior of Runtime Objects Using startObjectBehavior Actions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AutoStartActiveObjects3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic start of active objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;">System init Activity to start the Behavior of runtime objects using startObjectBehavior Actions.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="A Simplified System init Activity to be Used with autoStartActiveObjects Whose Value is True" ac:alt="A Simplified System init Activity to be Used with autoStartActiveObjects Whose Value is True"><ri:attachment ri:filename="AutoStartActiveObjects2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic start of active objects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"> A Simplified System init Activity to be used with autoStartActiveObjects whose value is true.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625212 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00281: (2026x) Behavior

- page_id: `255625212`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625212/2026x+Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:33.831+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Model simulation > (2026x) Elements simulation
- labels: ['behavior-simulation', 'simulating-behaviors']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1336333573

1336333575

1336333572

**On this page**

1336333574

You can simulate a Behavior (Activity, State-Machine, or Interaction) either with or without its context Classifier by simply right-clicking the Behavior in the browser or on a diagram and select either **Simulation** > **Run** or **Simulation** > **Run** **with** **Context**. Here, the classifier is the context of the Behavior.

You can also set the Behavior as a target of Simulation Configuration (See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running a Simulation Configuration' space=''] for more details) as follows

- Running a Behavior with its context Classifier If you run the Behavior with its context Classifier, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the object of the Classifier, which is the Behavior's context, to be simulated. And it will simulate the Behavior on the initialized object. If the Classifier has a Classifier Behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will start the Classifier Behavior of the context and wait until the object is in the idle state (waiting for a signal) to start the Behavior.
- Running a Behavior without a context Classifier If you run the Behavior without its context, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create an object of the Behavior and simulate it directly. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will also initialize values for the properties and parameters of the simulating Behavior. You can modify the values through the Variables pane before starting the simulation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The simulation of the Behavior with its properties and parameters.

To simulate a Behavior from an Activity, a State Machine diagram, or an Interaction directly, do either of the following

- Open the diagram and click Start on the Simulation window toolbar
- Right-click the diagram and select Simulation > Run .

The simulation of the Behavior, which is the context of the diagram, will run.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Here, the classifier is the context of the Behavior. </p><p>You can also set the Behavior as a target of Simulation Configuration (See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Running a Simulation Configuration" /></ac:link> for more details) as follows</p><ul><li><strong>Running</strong> <strong>a Behavior</strong> <strong>with</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>context Classifier</strong> <br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="06c31c4f-079d-45ae-9860-7c5ee2e8159f">If</ac:inline-comment-marker> you run the Behavior with its context Classifier, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the object of the Classifier, which is the Behavior's context, to be simulated. And it will simulate the Behavior on the initialized object. If the Classifier has a Classifier Behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will start the Classifier Behavior of the context <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fb1b2072-e01c-40db-b040-df8618ec2b31">and wait until the object is in the idle state (waiting for a signal) to start the Behavior.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <br /><br /></li><li><strong>Running</strong> <strong>a Behavior</strong> <strong>without</strong> <strong>a context Classifier</strong> <br />If you run the Behavior without its context, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create an object of the Behavior and simulate it directly. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will also initialize values for the properties and parameters of the simulating Behavior. You can modify the values through the <strong>Variables </strong>pane before starting the simulation. </li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:alt="The Behavior Simulation with Properties and Parameters" ac:title="The Behavior Simulation with Properties and Parameters"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_simulation_new.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behavior" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The simulation of the Behavior with its properties and parameters.</span></h6><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90277f73-a6e6-4bb5-b725-ee50ddd54965">simulate</ac:inline-comment-marker> a Behavior from an Activity, a State Machine diagram, or an Interaction directly, do either of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the diagram and click <strong>Start</strong> on the <strong>Simulation</strong> window toolbar</li><li>Right-click the diagram and select <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Run</strong>.</li></ul><p>The simulation of the Behavior, which is the context of the diagram, will run. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625619 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00282: (2026x) Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State

- page_id: `255625619`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625619/2026x+Behaviors+on+Entry+Exit+and+Do+Activities+of+a+State
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:52.063+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) State Machine simulation > (2026x) Adapting models for State Machine simulation
- labels: ['activity-state-behaviors', 'behaviors-of-activity-states']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

117294362

117294364

117294363

States can have defined Behaviors for any Activities: Entry, Exit, or Do Activity. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates a new simulation session to simulate those defined Behaviors. A defined Behavior can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior. The simulation engine corresponding to a defined Behavior will be used to simulate a model. If the defined Behavior is OpaqueBehavior, the ScriptEngine will be used to simulate the code in the body of OpaqueBehavior.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Behavior simulation on the entry of a State in the StopWatch.mdzip sample project.

According to Precise Semantics of UML State Machines (PSSM) Compatible CallEvent arguments handling, the Caller is suspended until the running to the completion step is finished in case of the Sync Call. The running to the completion step ends after the Entry or Do Activity is done. The input parameter must match the argument of the Entry or Do Activity. If it has an output parameter, it will be redefined until the running to the completion step is over. Therefore, the last output parameter is returned to the Caller.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Caller is suspended until the running to the completion step is completed according to PSSM Compatible CallEvent arguments handling.

117294361

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MT']

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit creates a new simulation session to simulate those defined Behaviors. A defined Behavior can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior. The simulation engine corresponding to a defined Behavior will be used to simulate a model. If the defined Behavior is OpaqueBehavior, the ScriptEngine will be used to simulate the code in the body of OpaqueBehavior. </p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Behavior Simulation on the Entry of a State (See Sample Project - StopWatch.mdzip)" ac:alt="The Behavior Simulation on the Entry of a State (See Sample Project - StopWatch.mdzip)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 66.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Behavior simulation on the entry of a State in the StopWatch.mdzip sample project.</h6><p>According to Precise Semantics of UML State Machines (PSSM) Compatible CallEvent arguments handling, the Caller is suspended until the running to the completion step is finished in case of the Sync Call. The running to the completion step ends after the Entry or Do Activity is done. The input parameter must match the argument of the Entry or Do Activity. If it has an output parameter, it will be redefined until the running to the completion step is over. Therefore, the last output parameter is returned to the Caller.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activity activities of a state.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Caller is suspended until the running to the completion step is completed according to PSSM Compatible CallEvent arguments handling.</h6><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95f593ec-0ae2-4f16-b116-088314a5efd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">117294361</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Opaque Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626355 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00283: (2026x) Binding Connector

- page_id: `255626355`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626355/2026x+Binding+Connector
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:40.762+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['binding-connector']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A binding Connector is a Connector to which a stereotype «BindingConnector» is applied. According to the SysML specification, a binding Connector specifies that properties at both ends of a Connector have equal values. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Value binding' space=''] for more information about value binding with a binding Connector.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A binding Connector is a Connector to which a stereotype «BindingConnector» is applied. According to the SysML specification, a binding Connector specifies that properties at both ends of a Connector have equal values. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Value binding" /></ac:link> for more information about value binding with a binding Connector. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625943 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00284: (2026x) Binding in a complex aggregate structure

- page_id: `255625943`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625943/2026x+Binding+in+a+complex+aggregate+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:16.784+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Value binding
- labels: ['binding-in-aggregate-structure']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A binding connector connecting deep nested properties in a block must apply a stereotype «NestedConnectorEnd» at both ends and specify the propertyPath (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Nested Connector end' space=''] for more information about the nested connector end). If there are multiple values specify a property in the propertyPath (the upper bound of the multiplicity of the property is greater than 1 or is infinite), Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will construct a value list at each end of the connector and maintain the values in the list. You can use (IsOrdered = true) to order each property in the propertyPath to ensure that the order of values on the list remains the same.

The following figure illustrates an example of binding where the aggregate structure is complex. It shows an executable InstanceSpecification of the block **System**, which is **system:System**. It has three instances of subsystems as the values of the slot **subsystem:Subsystem[0..**]**. In the SysML Parametric diagram, the value property *value** of the slot **subsystem:Subsystem[0..**]* are bound to the value property **value** of the block **System**. So, the values of the value property **value** of object **System** will be [25, 50, 75] respectively.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Value Binding in a Complex Aggregation Structure.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A binding connector connecting deep nested properties in a block must apply a stereotype «NestedConnectorEnd» at both ends and specify the propertyPath (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Nested Connector end" /></ac:link> for more information about the nested connector end). If there are multiple values specify a property in the propertyPath (the upper bound of the multiplicity of the property is greater than 1 or is infinite), Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will construct a value list at each end of the connector and maintain the values in the list. You can use (IsOrdered = true) to order each property in the propertyPath to ensure that the order of values on the list remains the same. </p><p>The following figure illustrates an example of binding where the aggregate structure is complex. It shows an executable InstanceSpecification of the block <strong>System</strong>, which is <strong>system:System</strong>. It has three instances of subsystems as the values of the slot <strong>subsystem:Subsystem[0..</strong>]<strong>. In the SysML Parametric diagram, the value property *value</strong> of the slot <strong>subsystem:Subsystem[0..</strong>]* are bound to the value property <strong>value</strong> of the block <strong>System</strong>. So, the values of the value property <strong>value</strong> of object <strong>System</strong> will be [25, 50, 75] respectively. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Value Binding in a Complex Aggregation Structure" ac:alt="Value Binding in a Complex Aggregation Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="complexAggre_binding.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding in a complex aggregate structure" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Value Binding in a Complex Aggregation Structure.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626353 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00285: (2026x) Block

- page_id: `255626353`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626353/2026x+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:40.559+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['block']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SysML block is a UML Class stereotyped with «Block». Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a SysML block the same way it simulates a UML Class.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A SysML block is a UML Class stereotyped with «Block». Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a SysML block the same way it simulates a UML Class. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624746 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00286: (2026x) BoundReference

- page_id: `255624746`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624746/2026x+BoundReference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:41:59.274+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['bound-reference']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

According to the SysML specification, a bound reference must have a binding connector to a property or a nested property of an owning block. Therefore, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will set the value of the bound reference so that it works with the properties connected with the binding connector. In exceptional circumstances, for example, if a Classifier that types a bound reference is a subtype of a Classifier that types a property at the other end of a binding connector, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the Classifier that types the bound reference to initialize the object. You can see more information about binding connector simulation in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding Connector' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Executing a Block Vehicle that contains Bound references.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>According to the SysML specification, a bound reference must have a binding connector to a property or a nested property of an owning block. Therefore, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will set the value of the bound reference so that it works with the properties connected with the binding connector. In exceptional circumstances, for example, if a Classifier that types a bound reference is a subtype of a Classifier that types a property at the other end of a binding connector, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the Classifier that types the bound reference to initialize the object. You can see more information about binding connector simulation in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding Connector" /></ac:link>. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Executing a Block Vehicle that Contains Bound References" ac:alt="Executing a Block Vehicle that Contains Bound References"><ri:attachment ri:filename="execBlockwithBoundRef.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) BoundReference" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Executing a Block Vehicle that contains Bound references.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625681 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00287: (2026x) Breakpoints

- page_id: `255625681`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625681/2026x+Breakpoints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:55.877+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation debugging
- labels: ['breakpoints']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

53874495

53874498

53874496

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to add or remove breakpoints to or from model elements. A model simulation will pause when these model elements are activated during the simulation. You can open the **Breakpoints** pane to see and manage all of the existing breakpoints in an active project. The **Breakpoints** pane lists all breakpoints with their properties shown in separate columns.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Breakpoints pane.

The Breakpoints pane columns are listed in the table as follows

| Column | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Enabled | To display the enabled or disabled state of a breakpoint. If the value is true, the breakpoint is enabled. Otherwise, the breakpoint is disabled. A model simulation will be suspended at that particular breakpoint only when the breakpoint is enabled (true). |
| Element | To represent a model element to which each breakpoint is applied. The model simulation will be suspended when the symbol of the element is activated or deactivated (depending on the value in the Suspend column). |
| Condition | To represent a breakpoint condition, a boolean expression, that will be evaluated when a model simulation reaches the element to which a breakpoint is applied. The simulation will be suspended at that particular element or breakpoint when the result of the boolean expression is true. If the condition is not defined, the simulation will always be suspended when it reaches that particular breakpoint. |
| Suspend | To suspend a model simulation. There are three kinds of simulation suspensions: On Entry, On Exit, and Both.On Entry to suspend simulation when a breakpoint's element is activated.On Exit to suspend simulation when a breakpoint's element is deactivated.Both to suspend simulation once a breakpoint's element is either activated or deactivated. |

53900734

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626013 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00288: (2026x) Built-in clock

- page_id: `255626013`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626013/2026x+Built-in+clock
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:22.553+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > (2026x) Simulation time and simulation clock
- labels: ['built-in-clock']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1349015512

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1349015514

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1349015513

The built-in clock generates simulation time continuously during a model simulation. You can use the clock ratio option in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to increase or decrease the amount of simulation time. The clock ratio specifies the ratio between actual time and simulation time in the same interval. You can set a ratio of the built-in clock for model simulation. The value you enter for the clock ratio tag definition of a Simulation Config allows you to scale a ratio up or down (see the following figure). For further information about stereotypes of the simulation configuration, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SimulationConfig stereotype' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Configuration with the specified clock ratio.

For example, if a clock ratio is 10, it means that 10 seconds current time is equivalent to one-second simulation time in the built-in clock. Therefore, the simulation clock is 10 times slower than the actual clock.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The current version of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to pause or resume the built-in clock.
- When you pause running simulation by either clicking the Suspend button or using breakpoints, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will stop the built-in clock operation and all running sessions temporarily. You can continue all of the activities by clicking the Resume button.

1349015511

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Internal simulation clock' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model-based clock' space='']

The value you enter for the clock ratio tag definition of a Simulation Config allows you to scale a ratio up or down (see the following figure). For further information about stereotypes of the simulation configuration, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SimulationConfig stereotype" /></ac:link>. </p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Simulation Configuration with the Specified Clock Ratio " ac:alt="The Simulation Configuration with the Specified Clock Ratio "><ri:attachment ri:filename="SimulationClock03.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Built-in clock" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The Simulation Configuration with the specified clock ratio.</span></h6><p>For example, if a clock ratio is 10, it means that 10 seconds current time is equivalent to one-second simulation time in the built-in clock. ## PAGE 00289: (2026x) Built-in Math

- page_id: `255626188`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626188/2026x+Built-in+Math
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:33.783+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator
- labels: ['built-in-math-solver']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

468817904

468817906

468817905

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit comes loaded with a built-in Math Solver. As a default Parametric Evaluator, Math Solver can solve simple mathematical and logical expressions. You can use it to evaluate as follows

- Mathematical and logical expressions defined in the Constraints of Constraint Blocks for Parametric Simulation on a SysML Parametric diagram.
- Mathematical and logical expressions in Simulation Console.

See also:

468817903

## PAGE 00290: (2026x) CallEvent

- page_id: `255626147`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626147/2026x+CallEvent
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:32.594+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Interaction simulation > (2026x) Supported elements in interaction simulation
- labels: ['call-event']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

380827074

380827076

380827075

A CallEvent is a type of Event in UML elements supported by Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit. A CallEvent of an Operation is triggered when you call an Operation with****a specified****Method. If an Operation is called without a Method, the CallEvent**will**be automatically generated. You can use the CallEvent on AcceptEventActions and Transitions**as shown in the following figures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A CallEvent on the AcceptEventActions of Actions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A CallEvent on the Transitions of States.

380827073

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MT']

##### Calling a specific behavior

You can use the following APIs to call any Behavior by its name or its qualified name.


If a found Behavior value is null or an argument's size is not equal to the in or input parameter's size of the found Behavior, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown.

##### Calling a specific operation

You can use the following APIs to call an operation by its name or its qualified name.

If the object is omitted then context (self) is used automatically.

You can also give initial parameter values in which the arguments lists must be created by executing the following code.


If a found Behavior value is null or an argument's size is not equal to the in or inout parameter's size of the found Behavior, an IllegalArgumentException will be thrown.

##### Creating list of arguments

A Java List will be required under certain circumstances, e.g., if you want to create Argument values that will be passed to some calling Behaviors or Operations.

The following code fragment shows how to create the Java ArrayList in the scripting language with ALH:


Alternatively, you can use an initial value as the constructor's parameter.


#### NOTE: Note

Note

- Only one initial value of the list is allowed.
- Variable-length argument lists are not supported.

````html
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42bbdb56-eb83-4ab2-bb12-f6653bf213c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If the object is omitted then context (self) is used automatically.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the following example, you can learn how to call operation &quot;test&quot; and return its value to variable &quot;result&quot;.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05a74076-308d-44aa-8e05-80b3ab61a463"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[result= ALH.callOperation("test");]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the following example, you can learn how to call a specified operation in ALH API by passing the operation's name and source port.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e79b5140-62be-471b-b4df-7fbd6bec7a27"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ ALH.callOperation("start", "p1")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can also give initial parameter values in which the arguments lists must be created by executing the following code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac9588b3-ae3d-4235-816f-dbb40dec694d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[arguments = ALH.createList();

arguments.add("value2");

arguemnts.add(20);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Alternatively, you can use an initial value as the constructor's parameter.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e76c6594-b78a-44be-ba12-fec5bb9410a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[arguments = ALH.createList(20);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68a1b4cd-7d9a-418d-b6e1-f390e77d35de"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Only one initial value of the list is allowed.</li><li>Variable-length argument lists are not supported.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
