Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p>To export a runtime value to an existing InstanceSpecification</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Do either of the following</p></li></ol><ul><li>Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the <strong>Name</strong> column and click the <strong>Export</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Instance</strong> icon on the <strong>Variables </strong>pane toolbar.</li><li>Right-click the row and select <strong>Export</strong> <strong>Value</strong> <strong>To</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance</strong>. </li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /><ac:image ac:title="Exporting a Runtime Valuetoan Existing Instance through the Context Menu" ac:alt="Exporting a Runtime Valuetoan Existing Instance through the Context Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_toExist_Inst.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d26878c-e8fa-4017-b2e2-51ed7ace483a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from a Classifier.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. The <strong>Select Instance</strong> dialog will open. Select an InstanceSpecification that will be used to save the runtime object (You can select only the InstanceSpecification whose classifier is the same as that of the runtime object).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Selecting an InstanceSpecification in the Select Instance Dialog" ac:alt="Selecting an InstanceSpecification in the Select Instance Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-instance-a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Click <strong>OK</strong>. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"> </span></p><p>To export a runtime value to the InstanceSpecification that has created the runtime object</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Do either of the following</p></li></ol><ul><li>Click a runtime object whose value you want to export in the <strong>Name</strong> column and click the <strong>Export</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Instance</strong> icon on the <strong>Variables</strong> pane toolbar.</li><li>Right-click the row and select <strong>Export</strong> <strong>Value</strong> <strong>To</strong> &gt; <strong>Instance</strong>. </li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Exporting a Runtime Value to the Creating Instance through the Context Menu" ac:alt="Exporting a Runtime Value to the Creating Instance through the Context Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_toCreatorInstance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Once Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit exported and saved the values of the runtime object to the Instance Specification that created it, you can see the hyperlink to that particular Instance Specification in the <strong>Simulation</strong> <strong>Console</strong> pane.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Successful Export Notification and Hyperlink" ac:alt="Successful Export Notification and Hyperlink"><ri:attachment ri:filename="110150_2016-06-28+12_07_54-Greenshot.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exporting runtime objects to InstanceSpecifications" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Click the hyperlink, and you will see the exact Instance Specification in the Containment tree.</p><p>This method allows you to export the value of the runtime object to the slots of the Instance Specification that has created the object. You can see the notification of each successful export and a location hyperlink, if any, in the <strong>Simulation</strong> <strong>Console</strong> pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a159bd45-9c64-400a-a3c9-db306df49176"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export the value of a runtime object using this method, the runtime object must be created from an Instance Specification.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f7485fa-3963-4103-a495-e6f04f35f800"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">45332698</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instance Specification" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Simulation console" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Variables pane" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624817 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00357: (2026x) Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements

- page_id: `255624817`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624817/2026x+Extraction+of+Constraints+from+Text+Based+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:05.612+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Requirements traceability from the Variables pane
- labels: ['extracting-constraints-from-text-based-requirements']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of version 18.4, another new addition to Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit's already impressive repertoire, is the ability to automatically extract constraint equations from the text of a requirement. 
For example, if a requirement text states that a moving car must have speed more than 0, then the constraint equation “speed > 0” is automatically extracted from the requirement text and will be evaluated upon running of a simulation. 
While typing the requirement text, a shortcut menu appears as soon as you type some keywords which are available in the Glossary, see the example as follows

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Constraints within text-based requirements.

For the constraint to execute properly, the requirement should be linked to a property, such as in the above example, a satisfy relation is used to link between the property and the requirement.

When the simulation is run, the constraint is evaluated and color-coded according to the result of the simulation, red if the constraint fails and green if it passes, as shown in figure below. Additionally, a mouse over the variable will display a tooltip.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>As of version 18.4, another new addition to Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit's already impressive repertoire, is the ability to automatically extract constraint equations from the text of a requirement.<br />For example, if a requirement text states that a moving car must have speed more than 0, then the constraint equation “speed &gt; 0” is automatically extracted from the requirement text and will be evaluated upon running of a simulation.<br />While typing the requirement text, a shortcut menu appears as soon as you type some keywords which are available in the Glossary, see the example as follows</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Constraints within text based  requirements" ac:alt="Constraints within text based  requirements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="108924_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Constraints within text-based requirements.</h6><p>For the constraint to execute properly, the requirement should be linked to a property, such as in the above example, a satisfy relation is used to link between the property and the requirement.</p><p>When the simulation is run, the constraint is evaluated and color-coded according to the result of the simulation, red if the constraint fails and green if it passes, as shown in figure below. Additionally, a mouse over the variable will display a tooltip. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it" ac:alt="Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it"><ri:attachment ri:filename="108925_b.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extraction of Constraints from Text Based Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Running a simulation evaluates the constraint within the requirement and color-codes it.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625484 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00358: (2026x) Flow Port

- page_id: `255625484`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625484/2026x+Flow+Port
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:24.003+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements > (2026x) Port
- labels: ['flow-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

944174807

944174818

944174808

A [Flow Port](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Flow+Port) is a Port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow updating depends on directions (**in**, **out**, and **inout** directions as shown in the figure below). If there is the **in** or **out** direction, the value cannot be updated in the reverse direction. In general, Flow Ports are used for asynchronous, broadcast, and send-and-forget interactions. Animation of the value flow is animated through parts, Ports, and Connectors. Both atomic Flow Ports (typed by Signal or ValueType) and non-atomic Flow Ports (typed by FlowSpecification) are supported. If the Flow Port is connected to multiple external and/or internal Connectors, the items are propagated (broadcast) to the other ends of all Connectors that have matching properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Flow Ports with animation of value propagation in the Internal Block diagram.

944174806

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Port' space='']
- Full Port
- Proxy Port

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7f440aa-f244-46c4-883a-da53d49e1d67"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174807</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e60bd445-1abc-4057-a8f3-dcf891f706a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174818</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ae9ba71-5c7e-440b-bbd3-7c901cbc9161"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174808</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Flow+Port">Flow Port</a> is a Port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow updating depends on directions (<strong>in</strong>, <strong>out</strong>, and <strong>inout</strong> directions as shown in the figure below). If there is the <strong>in</strong> or <strong>out</strong> direction, the value cannot be updated in the reverse direction. In general, Flow Ports are used for asynchronous, broadcast, and send-and-forget interactions. Animation of the value flow is animated through parts, Ports, and Connectors. Both atomic Flow Ports (typed by Signal or ValueType) and non-atomic Flow Ports (typed by FlowSpecification) are supported. If the Flow Port is connected to multiple external and/or internal Connectors, the items are propagated (broadcast) to the other ends of all Connectors that have matching properties.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Flow Port.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Flow Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Flow Ports with animation of value propagation in the Internal Block diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f841c696-fc2d-400d-b74d-5813886768fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944174806</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Port" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Full+Port">Full Port</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Proxy+Port">Proxy Port</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624741 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00359: (2026x) Flow property

- page_id: `255624741`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624741/2026x+Flow+property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:41:58.754+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['flow-property']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A flow property is a property to which the stereotype «FlowProperty» is applied. A flow property has a flow direction. A flow due to flow properties will incur flow properties matching. Matching flow properties must have the same direction and type. If the direction and type of multiple flow properties at either end of a connector match, a flow will occur between the flow properties with the same name. When the flow occurs between matching flow properties, the value of the flow property with a direction 'out' or 'inout' will be delivered to the flow property with the direction 'in' or 'inout'.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A flow property is a property to which the stereotype «FlowProperty» is applied. A flow property has a flow direction. A flow due to flow properties will incur flow properties matching. Matching flow properties must have the same direction and type. If the direction and type of multiple flow properties at either end of a connector match, a flow will occur between the flow properties with the same name. When the flow occurs between matching flow properties, the value of the flow property with a direction 'out' or 'inout' will be delivered to the flow property with the direction 'in' or 'inout'. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626383 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00360: (2026x) FMI 2.0 co-simulation

- page_id: `255626383`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626383/2026x+FMI+2.0+co-simulation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:24.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models
- labels: ['fmi-co-simulation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) Version 1.0 and 2.0. FMI 2.0 co-simulation applies when several FMIs are connected in Internal Block diagrams (IBD), an integration of separate independent black box simulation models along with the master algorithm.

FMI is a standard that supports model exchange and co-simulation of models. A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mockup Unit). The Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is also capable of reading FMU files. An FMU is a zipped file containing an XML description file and implementation in binary form that executes the equation representing the component’s Behavior.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Before simulating FMU files, you must make sure the following conditions are met:

- The FMU file can be attached to the project, placed in the same directory as the project or the path to the file can be specified as url tag on the FMU block. The path is set automatically when importing an FMU file. [ATTACHMENT filename='File path .png']
- The FMU files must be OS-platform supported, e.g., Win64, Win32, linux64, linux32, etc. (Cross-platform execution of FMU files is not supported.). You can see all supported platforms in the Binaries folders of the FMU files. If the platform you are using is not in the Binaries folder, it will not be supported.
- An FMU Block requires a SimulationConfig with the Internal simulation clock to provide the reference time in the simulation (shown in Step 3 below). Otherwise, a warning message will be shown in the Console pane. Please see also the WaterTankFMI build-in sample of Simulation.

To co-simulate FMU Blocks

1. Drag FMU files into a Block Definition diagram of the SysML project. The FMU files will be presented as «block» and «fmu» stereotypes along with ports. You may create associations (aggregations) with the main system block to make the FMU files become part properties.
2. If there is only one FMU file to simulate, proceed to Step 3. Otherwise, you will have to create an Internal Block diagram for the main system block to automatically include part properties in the diagram. Select the Flow Port context menu to show flow ports of those properties and create connections as needed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

3. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram with a [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype) to specify *startTime, stepSize*, and *endTime* (optional) in the properties. You can add a [Time series chart](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Time+series+chart) and specify *represents* and *value* of the FMU represented as user interfaces. You can also include [CSVExport](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) CSV+export) to store the results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

4. Run the Simulation Configuration and see the results in the Time series chart and **Variables** pane, as well as the resulting Excel file from CSVExport.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Textbody">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) Version 1.0 and 2.0. FMI 2.0 co-simulation applies when several FMIs are connected in Internal Block diagrams (IBD), an integration of separate independent black box simulation models along with the master algorithm.</p><p class="Textbody">FMI is a standard that supports model exchange and co-simulation of models. A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mockup Unit). The Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is also capable of reading FMU files. An FMU is a zipped file containing an XML description file and implementation in binary form that executes the equation representing the component’s Behavior.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a71e4f85-9e60-4ce5-9c23-3378be2dc56a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="Textbody">Before simulating FMU files, you must make sure the following conditions are met:</p><ul><li>The FMU file can be attached to the project, placed in the same directory as the project or the path to the file can be specified as url tag on the FMU block. The path is set automatically when importing an FMU file.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="File path .png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMI 2.0 co-simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>The FMU files must be OS-platform supported, e.g., Win64, Win32, linux64, linux32, etc. (Cross-platform execution of FMU files is not supported.). You can see all supported platforms in the <strong>Binaries</strong> folders of the FMU files. If the platform you are using is not in the <strong>Binaries</strong> folder, it will not be supported.</li><li>An FMU Block requires a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a> with the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Internal+simulation+clock">Internal simulation clock</a> to provide the reference time in the simulation (shown in Step 3 below). Otherwise, a warning message will be shown in the <strong>Console</strong> pane. Please see also the <strong>WaterTankFMI</strong> build-in sample of Simulation.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To co-simulate FMU Blocks</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag FMU files into a Block Definition diagram of the SysML project. The FMU files will be presented as «block» and «fmu» stereotypes along with ports. You may create associations (aggregations) with the main system block to make the FMU files become part properties.</li><li>If there is only one FMU file to simulate, proceed to Step 3. Otherwise, you will have to create an Internal Block diagram for the main system block to automatically include part properties in the diagram. Select the <strong>Flow Port</strong> context menu to show flow ports of those properties and create connections as needed.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-1 Creating  a SysML Internal Block Diagram.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMI 2.0 co-simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram with a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a> to specify <em>startTime, stepSize</em>, and <em>endTime</em> (optional) in the properties. You can add a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Time+series+chart">Time series chart</a> and specify <em>represents</em> and <em>value</em> of the FMU represented as user interfaces. You can also include <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) CSV+export">CSVExport</a> to store the results.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-2 Creating a Simulation Configuration Diagram.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMI 2.0 co-simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">4. Run the Simulation Configuration and see the results in the Time series chart and <strong>Variables</strong> pane, as well as the resulting Excel file from CSVExport.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7113-3 Running the Simulation Configuration.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMI 2.0 co-simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625412 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00361: (2026x) Frequently asked questions

- page_id: `255625412`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625412/2026x+Frequently+asked+questions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:29.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['frequently-asked-questions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1155794510

1155794521

1155794509

**On this page**

1155794520

##### Activity simulation

**Question 1**:

Why isn't an operation outside a context object executed, e.g., an operation referring to an Activity outside a Block?

**Answer**: According to the fUML specification, an operation will be dispatched to the target object only, which is the context of the execution. You need to move the Activity into the Block and use it as a CallBehaviorAction with the Behavior setting.

**Question 2**:

Can I simulate dummy tokens of CallBehaviorActions that do not have a Behavior?

**Answer**: Yes, you can set the **Auto-Create fUML Object of Output Pin** option in the **Simulation [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project options' space='']**dialog to **true**, which is the default value in Version 19.0 SP2 and later. Please see also****[Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Dummy+tokens+of+Actions+through+Output+Pins).

##### Parametric evaluation

**Question 1**:

Why can't I use MATLAB as an external evaluator even after [integration with MATLAB](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Integration+with+MATLAB) of the modeling tool?

**Answer**: You must use the 64-bit version of MATLAB (Version 2012a or later) to align it with the 64-bit version of the modeling tool you are working with, e.g., MagicDraw or Cameo Systems Modeler. According to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using MATLAB on Microsoft Windows' space=''], you must also ensure that **Path** of **System variables** in the **Environment Variables** dialog is set to the correct MATLAB path.

**Question 2**:

Can I use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator?

**Answer**: Yes, you can use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator. MATLAB R2019b has a new option, **Single simulation output**, set true by default in Simulink, which doesn’t affect the simulation. However, there are a few limitations as follows:

- If Simulation cannot load the Simulink, e.g., an invalid URL/ filename, both warning and script exception messages will be printed in the Console panel. In this case, the MATLAB engine cannot be used, so you will need to manually restart MATLAB.
- If there is no input for the In Port, Simulation will skip the evaluation, and a warning message will be printed in Console panel.

##### State Machine simulation

**Question**:

Can I change State activation semantics from **before entry** (official UML semantics) to **after entry**?

**Answer**: Yes, you can set the **State** **Activation** **Semantics** option in the **Simulation [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project options' space='']**dialog to **After entry**. The default value is **Before entry** in Version 19.0 and later. Please see also [State activation semantics.](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) State+activation+semantics)

1775218708

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de4adf70-1b0b-4eb8-8bed-afa6fb403dec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794510</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ccf0c90-ccff-43bf-a060-d506b1d812d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794521</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b424aca-9031-4ecf-ba3b-4dd88ceecd4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794509</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e37618d-f862-452f-ba10-5b92eb5a88e0" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6749c4a8-6a81-4078-8ffa-1aba66cc2892"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1155794520</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Activity simulation</h3><p><strong>Question 1</strong>:</p><p>Why isn't an operation outside a context object executed, e.g., an operation referring to an Activity outside a Block?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: According to the fUML specification, an operation will be dispatched to the target object only, which is the context of the execution. You need to move the Activity into the Block and use it as a CallBehaviorAction with the Behavior setting.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Question 2</strong>:</p><p>Can I simulate dummy tokens of CallBehaviorActions that do not have a Behavior?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can set the <strong>Auto-Create fUML Object of Output Pin</strong> option in the <strong>Simulation <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog to <strong>true</strong>, which is the default value in Version 19.0 SP2 and later. Please see also<strong> </strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Dummy+tokens+of+Actions+through+Output+Pins">Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins</a>.</p><p><br /></p><hr /><h3>Parametric evaluation</h3><p><strong>Question 1</strong>:</p><p>Why can't I use MATLAB as an external evaluator even after <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Integration+with+MATLAB">integration with MATLAB</a> of the modeling tool?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: You must use the 64-bit version of MATLAB (Version 2012a or later) to align it with the 64-bit version of the modeling tool you are working with, e.g., MagicDraw or Cameo Systems Modeler. According to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using MATLAB on Microsoft Windows" /></ac:link>, you must also ensure that <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Path</strong> of <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">System variables</strong> in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Environment Variables</strong> dialog is set to the correct MATLAB path<span class="external-link" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><p><span class="external-link"><br /></span></p><p><strong>Question 2</strong>:</p><p>Can I use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can use MATLAB 2019b as an external evaluator. MATLAB R2019b has a new option, <strong>Single simulation output</strong>, set true by default in Simulink, which doesn’t affect the simulation. However, there are a few limitations as follows:</p><ul><li>If Simulation cannot load the Simulink, e.g., an invalid URL/ filename, both warning and script exception messages will be printed in the <strong>Console</strong> panel. In this case, the MATLAB engine cannot be used, so you will need to manually restart MATLAB.</li><li>If there is no input for the In Port, Simulation will skip the evaluation, and a warning message will be printed in <strong>Console</strong> panel.</li></ul><p><span class="external-link"><br /></span></p><hr /><h3>State Machine simulation</h3><p><strong>Question</strong>:</p><p>Can I change State activation semantics from <strong>before entry</strong> (official UML semantics) to <strong>after entry</strong>?</p><p><strong>Answer</strong>: Yes, you can set the <strong>State</strong> <strong>Activation</strong> <strong>Semantics</strong> option in the <strong>Simulation <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog to <strong>After entry</strong>. The default value is <strong>Before entry</strong> in Version 19.0 and later. Please see also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) State+activation+semantics">State activation semantics.</a></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57e61c02-fb9a-4963-8dc0-ee001ec98766"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1775218708</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Activity simulation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Block" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Call Behavior Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Integration with MATLAB" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Parametric evaluator" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) State activation semantics" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dummy tokens of Actions through Output Pins" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625481 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00362: (2026x) Full Port

- page_id: `255625481`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625481/2026x+Full+Port
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:23.875+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements > (2026x) Port
- labels: ['full-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

944179700

944179711

944179710

A Full port is a port stereotyped with «FullPort». It specifies a separate Element of an owning block. Initializing the owning block will cause Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to initialize a port object from a Classifier that types the full port.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
An Object containing full Ports.

944179699

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow Port' space='']
- Proxy Port

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fc5b9c0-7516-4a97-8e1d-a95a05e447a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944179700</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56e83bec-238e-47ad-923a-c66f646cd89c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944179711</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21a1e0fc-3e3c-4dad-978c-bcb2750476b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944179710</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Full port is a port stereotyped with «FullPort». It specifies a separate Element of an owning block. Initializing the owning block will cause Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to initialize a port object from a Classifier that types the full port. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="An Object Containing Full Ports" ac:alt="An Object Containing Full Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="objectwithFullports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Full Port" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />An Object containing full Ports.</h6><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8c20ae5-aee9-4dd0-952b-afef44f1ddbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">944179699</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Port" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Flow Port" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Proxy+Port">Proxy Port</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626190 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00363: (2026x) Functions

- page_id: `255626190`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626190/2026x+Functions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:33.888+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Built-in Math
- labels: ['functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- x and y represent real values or variables.

- c and d represent complex values or variables.

- m and n represent integer values or variables.

- U represents a matrix of values.

- A matrix can be passed to a function that operates Element-wise on matrices, as its argument in the example as follows

X = [1, -2, 3; -4, 5, -6; 7, -8, 9];

Y = abs(X) 
result: 
Y = [1 2 3; 4 5 6; 7 8 9]

- det(U) - To return a determinant of a given matrix. U is a square matrix.

| Function name | Syntax | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| abs | abs(x)abs(c) | To return an absolute value of x or a complex modulus of c. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acos | acos(x)acos(c) | To return an arc cosine of an angle in the range of 0.0 through pi. All angles are measured in radians.This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acosd | acosd(x)acosd(c) | To return an inverse cosine of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acosh | acosh(x)acosh(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic cosine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acot | acot(x)acot(c) | To return an inverse cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acotd | acotd(x)acotd(c) | To return an inverse cotangent of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acoth | acoth(x)acoth(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acsc | acsc(x)acsc(c) | To return an inverse cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acscd | acscd(x)acscd(c) | To return an inverse cosecant of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| acsch | acsch(x)acsch(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asec | asec(x)asec(c) | To return an inverse secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asecd | asecd(x)asecd(c) | To return an inverse secant of a given value expressed in degrees.This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asech | asech(x)asech(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asin | asin(x)asin(c) | To return an arc sine of an angle in the range of -pi/2 through pi/2. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asind | asind(x)asind(c) | To return an inverse sine of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| asinh | asinh(x)asinh(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic sine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| atan | atan(x)atan(c) | To return an arc tangent of an angle in the range of -pi/2 through pi/2. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| atan2 | atan2(x,y)atan2(U,V) | To return an arc tangent of an angle in the range of -pi through pi. atan2(U, V) returns a matrix of the same size as the U and V matrices containing the Element-by-Element, inverse tangent of the real parts of U and V. |
| atand | atand(x)atand(c) | To return an inverse tangent of a given value, expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| atanh | atanh(x)atanh(c) | To return an inverse hyperbolic tangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| ceil | ceil(x) | To return a smallest (closest to negative infinity) value that is not less than the value of x and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| col | col(U, m) | To return a specific column values of a given matrix. U is a square matrix, m is a column index (starts at 0). |
| conj | conj(c) | To return a conjugated value of c. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cos | cos(x)cos(c) | To return a trigonometric cosine of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cosd | cosd(x)cosd(c) | To return a cosine of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cosh | cosh(x)cosh(c) | To return a hyperbolic cosine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cot | cot(x)cot(c) | To return a cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cotd | cotd(x)cotd(c) | To return a cotangent of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| coth | coth(x)coth(c) | To return a hyperbolic cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| count | count(U) | To return a number of Elements of a given matrix. |
| csc | csc(x)csc(c) | To return a cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| cscd | cscd(x)cscd(c) | To return a cosecant of a given value expressed in degree. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| csch | csch(x)csch(c) | To return a hyperbolic cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| det | det(U) | To return a determinant of a given matrix. U is a square matrix. |
| diag | diag(U)diag(U,m) | To return a diagonal matrix and diagonals of the matrix. If U is a row matrix or a column matrix of n Elements, this function will return a square matrix of order n+abs(m), with the Elements of U on the kth diagonal. k = 0 represents the main diagonal. k > 0 is above the main diagonal. k < 0 is below the main diagonal. If U is a square matrix, this function will return a column matrix formed by the Elements of the kth diagonal of U. |
| exp | exp(x)exp(c) | To return a Euler's number e raised to the power of a or c. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| eye | eye(m) | To return an identity matrix of dimension m x m. |
| factorial | factorial(m) | To return a factorial of m value. |
| floor | floor(x)floor(c) | To return a largest (closest to positive infinity) value that is not greater than the value of x and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| IEEEremainder | IEEEremainder(x,y) | To compute the remainder operation in two arguments as prescribed by the IEEE 754 standard. |
| if | if(b,x,y) | To return the value of x if b is true. Otherwise, y is returned. Where b is a boolean value. |
| imag | imag(c) | To return a real value of an imaginary part of a given complex number. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| invert | invert(U) | To return an inverse or pseudo inverse of a given matrix. If the given matrix is a square matrix, the inverse of a U matrix will be returned using the LU factorization. If the given matrix is not a square matrix, a pseudo inverse matrix will be returned using the QR factorization. |
| linsolve | linsolve(U,V) | X = linsolve(U,V) solves the linear system U*X = V using the LU factorization with partial pivoting when U is a square matrix. |
| In | ln(x)ln(c) | To return a natural logarithm (base e) of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| log | log(x, y)log(c) | To return the logarithm (base x) of a given value y. If only one parameter is provided, the function operates with the base of e (natural logarithm). This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| log10 | log10(x)log10(c) | To return a logarithm base 10 of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| log2 | log2(x)log2(c) | To return a logarithm base 2 of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| max | max(x,y,...)max(c,d,...)max(U)max(U,V) | To return a greater of the given values. max(U) returns the largest Element of a given matrix. max(U, V) returns a matrix the same size as U and V with the largest Elements taken from U or V. The dimensions of U and V must be the same. |
| mean | mean(U) | To return a mean or average value of a given matrix. U is a row or a column matrix: mean(U) returns the mean value of all Elements in the given matrix.U is a 2-D matrix: mean(U) returns a row matrix that contains the mean value of each column of the given matrix. |
| median | median(U) | To return a median value of a given matrix. U is a row or column matrix: median(U) returns the median value of all Elements in the given matrix. U is a 2-D matrix: median(U) returns a row matrix that contains the median value of each column of the given matrix. |
| min | min(x,y,...)min(c,d,...)min(U)min(U,V) | To return a smaller of the given values. min(U) returns the smallest Element of a given matrix. min(U, V) returns a matrix the same size as U and V with the smallest Elements taken from U or V. The dimensions of U and V must be the same. |
| mod | mod(x, y) | To return floored division modulus of number x with divisor y. The returned number is always the same sign as a divisor. |
| num2str | num2str(x)num2str(c) | To return a string specifying a given number x. |
| ones | ones(m,n) | To return an m x n matrix of all 1s. |
| pow | pow(x, y)pow(U, c)pow(c, d)pow(U, x)pow(x, U) | To return a value of the first argument raised to the power of the second argument. This function operates Element-wise on a given matrix U. |
| random | random() | To return a real value with a positive sign, greater than or equal to 0.0 but less than 1.0. |
| real | real(c) | To return a real value of the real part of a given complex number. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| rint | rint(x) | To return a value that is closest in value to an argument and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| round | round(x) | To return a closest value to an argument and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| row | row(U, m) | To return a specific row values of a given matrix. U is a square matrix, m is a row index (starts at 0). |
| sec | sec(x)sec(c) | To return a secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| secd | secd(x)secd(c) | To return a secant of a given value expressed in degree. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| sech | sech(x)sech(c) | To return a hyperbolic secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| sin | sin(x)sin(c) | To return a trigonometric sine of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| sind | sind(x)sind(c) | To return a sine of a given value, expressed in degree This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| sinh | sinh(x)sinh(c) | To return a hyperbolic sine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| size | size(U)size(U, m) | To return a size of a given matrix. If only the matrix is passed to the function as an argument, the returned value is a 1x2 matrix. The first Element is the number of rows and the second Element is the number of columns.If the second parameter (m) is specified, this function will return the size of an mth dimension of a given matrix as a scalar value. The second argument can be 1 or 2 (1 for the row size and 2 for the column size). For example:U = [1, 2, 3; 4, 5, 6];size(U) is [2, 3]size(U, 1) is 2size(U, 2) is 3 |
| sort | sort(U)sort(U, ‘descend’) | To sort the Elements of a given matrix in an ascending or descending order. If the second argument is specified with ‘ascend’ or ‘descend’, the Elements will be in an ascending or descending order respectively. If this function is called without a second argument, the Elements will be sorted in an ascending order.U is a row or column matrix: sort(U) and sort(U, ascend) sort all Elements in the given matrix. U is a 2-D matrix: sort(U) and sort(U, ascend) sort Elements in each column of the given matrix. |
| ssqrt | sqrt(x)sqrt(c) | To return a correctly rounded positive square root of a double value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| std | std(U)std(U, flag) | To return a standard deviation of a given matrix. The ’flag’ argument can be 0 or 1. It specifies the method for calculating the standard deviation. If the flag = 0, the standard deviation is normalized by N-1. If the flag = 1, the standard deviation is normalized by N where N is the number of data. The value of the flag will be zero by default. U is a row or column matrix: std(U) and std(U, flag) returns the standard deviation of all Elements in the given matrix. U is 2-D matrix: std(U) and std(U,flag) returns a row matrix that contains the standard deviation of each column of the given matrix. |
| str2num | str2num(s) | To return a number specified by a given string s. |
| sum | sum(U) | To return a summation of all Elements in a U matrix. |
| tan | tan(x)tan(c) | To return a trigonometric tangent of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| tand | tand(x)tand(c) | To return a tangent of a given value expressed in degree.This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| tanh | tanh(x)tanh(c) | To return a hyperbolic tangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| toDegrees | toDegrees(x)toDegrees(c) | To convert an angle measured in radians to an approximately equivalent angle measured in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| toRadians |  | To convert an angle measured in degrees to an approximately equivalent angle measured in radians. This function operates Element-wise on matrices. |
| transpose | transpose(U) | To return a transposition of a given matrix |
| variance | variance(U) | To return a variance of a given matrix. U is a row or column matrix:variance(U) returns the variance of all Elements in the given matrix. U is a 2-D matrix: variance(U) returns a row matrix that contains the variance of each column of the given matrix. |
| zeros | zeros(m, n) | To return an m x n matrix of all 0s. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>asinh</td><td><p>asinh(x)</p><p>asinh(c)</p></td><td><p>To return an inverse hyperbolic sine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>atan</td><td><p>atan(x)</p><p>atan(c)</p></td><td><p>To return an arc tangent of an angle in the range of -pi/2 through pi/2. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>atan2</td><td><p><span>atan2(x,y)</span></p><p><span><span>atan2(U,V)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return an arc tangent of an angle in the range of -pi through pi. atan2(U, V) returns a matrix of the same size as the U and V matrices containing the Element-by-Element, inverse tangent of the real parts of U and V.</p></td></tr><tr><td>atand</td><td><p><span>atand(x)</span></p><p><span><span>atand(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return an inverse tangent of a given value, expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>atanh</td><td><p><span>atanh(x)</span></p><p><span><span>atanh(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return an inverse hyperbolic tangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>ceil</td><td>ceil(x)</td><td><p>To return a smallest (closest to negative infinity) value that is not less than the value of x and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">col</span></td><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">col(U, m)</span></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To return a specific column values of a given matrix. U is a square matrix, m is a column index (starts at 0).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>conj</td><td>conj(c)</td><td><p>To return a conjugated value of c. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cos</td><td><p><span>cos(x)</span></p><p><span><span>cos(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a trigonometric cosine of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cosd</td><td><p><span>cosd(x)</span></p><p><span><span>cosd(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a cosine of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cosh</td><td><p><span>cosh(x)</span></p><p><span><span>cosh(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic cosine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cot</td><td><p><span>cot(x)</span></p><p><span>cot(c)</span></p></td><td><p>To return a cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cotd</td><td><p>cotd(x)</p><p>cotd(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a cotangent of a given value expressed in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>coth</td><td><p>coth(x)</p><p>coth(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic cotangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>count</td><td>count(U)</td><td>To return a number of Elements of a given matrix.</td></tr><tr><td>csc</td><td><p>csc(x)</p><p>csc(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>cscd</td><td><p><span>cscd(x)</span></p><p><span><span>cscd(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a cosecant of a given value expressed in degree. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>csch</td><td><p><span>csch(x)</span></p><p><span><span>csch(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic cosecant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">det</span></td><td><p><span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">det(U)</span></span></p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To return a determinant of a given matrix. U is a square matrix.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>diag</td><td><p>diag(U)</p><p>diag(U,m)</p></td><td><p>To return a diagonal matrix and diagonals of the matrix. If U is a row matrix or a column matrix of n Elements, this function will return a square matrix of order n+abs(m), with the Elements of U on the kth <br />diagonal. k = 0 represents the main <br />diagonal. k &gt; 0 is above the main <br />diagonal. k &lt; 0 is below the main <br />diagonal. If U is a square matrix, this function will return a column matrix formed by the Elements of the kth diagonal of U.</p></td></tr><tr><td>exp</td><td><p>exp(x)</p><p>exp(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a Euler's number e raised to the power of a or c. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>eye</td><td>eye(m)</td><td>To return an identity matrix of dimension m x m.</td></tr><tr><td>factorial</td><td>factorial(m)</td><td>To return a factorial of m value.</td></tr><tr><td>floor</td><td><p>floor(x)</p><p>floor(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a largest (closest to positive infinity) value that is not greater than the value of x and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function <br />operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>IEEEremainder</td><td><span>IEEEremainder(x,y)</span></td><td>To compute the remainder operation in two arguments as prescribed by the IEEE 754 standard.</td></tr><tr><td>if</td><td>if(b,x,y)</td><td>To return the value of x if b is true. Otherwise, y is returned. Where b is a boolean value.</td></tr><tr><td>imag</td><td>imag(c)</td><td><p>To return a real value of an imaginary part of a given complex number. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>invert</td><td>invert(U)</td><td><p>To return an inverse or pseudo inverse of a given matrix. If the given matrix is a square matrix, the inverse of a U matrix will be returned using the LU factorization. If the given matrix is not a square matrix, a pseudo inverse matrix will be returned using the QR factorization.</p></td></tr><tr><td>linsolve</td><td>linsolve(U,V)</td><td>X = linsolve(U,V) solves the linear system <br />U*X = V using the LU factorization with partial pivoting when U is a square matrix.</td></tr><tr><td>In</td><td><p>ln(x)</p><p>ln(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a natural logarithm (base e) of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>log</td><td><p>log(x, y)</p><p>log(c)</p></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To return the logarithm (base x) of a given value y. If only one parameter is provided, the function operates with the base of e (natural logarithm). This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>log10</td><td><p><span>log10(x)</span></p><p><span><span>log10(c)</span></span></p></td><td><p>To return a logarithm base 10 of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>log2</td><td><p>log2(x)</p><p>log2(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a logarithm base 2 of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>max</td><td><p>max(x,y,...)</p><p>max(c,d,...)</p><p>max(U)</p><p>max(U,V)</p></td><td><p>To return a greater of the given values. max(U) returns the largest Element of a given matrix. max(U, V) returns a matrix the same size as U and V with the largest Elements taken from U or V. The dimensions of U and V must be the same.</p></td></tr><tr><td>mean</td><td>mean(U)</td><td><p>To return a mean or average value of a given matrix. U is a row or a column matrix: mean(U) returns the mean value of all Elements in the given matrix.U is a 2-D matrix: mean(U) returns a row matrix that contains the mean value of each column of the given matrix.</p></td></tr><tr><td>median</td><td>median(U)</td><td><p>To return a median value of a given matrix. U is a row or column matrix: median(U) <br />returns the median value of all Elements in the given matrix. U is a 2-D matrix: median(U) returns a row matrix that contains the median value of each column of the given matrix.</p></td></tr><tr><td>min</td><td><p>min(x,y,...)</p><p>min(c,d,...)</p><p>min(U)</p><p>min(U,V)</p></td><td><p>To return a smaller of the given values. min(U) returns the smallest Element of a given matrix. min(U, V) returns a matrix the same size as U and V with the smallest Elements taken from U or V. The dimensions of U and V must be the same.</p></td></tr><tr><td>mod</td><td><p>mod(x, y)</p></td><td><p>To return floored division modulus of number x with divisor y. The returned number is always the same sign as a divisor.</p></td></tr><tr><td>num2str</td><td><p><span>num2str(x)</span></p><p><span><span>num2str(c)</span></span></p></td><td>To return a string specifying a given number x.</td></tr><tr><td>ones</td><td>ones(m,n)</td><td>To return an m x n matrix of all 1s.</td></tr><tr><td>pow</td><td><p>pow(x, y)<br />pow(U, c)<br />pow(c, d)<br />pow(U, x)<br />pow(x, U)</p></td><td><p>To return a value of the first argument raised to the power of the second argument. This function operates Element-wise on a given matrix U.</p></td></tr><tr><td>random</td><td>random()</td><td>To return a real value with a positive sign, greater than or equal to 0.0 but less than 1.0.</td></tr><tr><td>real</td><td>real(c)</td><td><p>To return a real value of the real part of a given complex number. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>rint</td><td>rint(x)</td><td><p>To return a value that is closest in value to an argument and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>round</td><td>round(x)</td><td><p>To return a closest value to an argument and is equal to a mathematical integer. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">row</span></td><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">row(U, m)</span></td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To return a specific row values of a given matrix. U is a square matrix, m is a row index (starts at 0).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>sec</td><td><p>sec(x)<br />sec(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>secd</td><td><p>secd(x)<br />secd(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a secant of a given value expressed in degree. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>sech</td><td><p>sech(x)<br />sech(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic secant of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>sin</td><td><p>sin(x)<br />sin(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a trigonometric sine of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>sind</td><td><p>sind(x)</p><p>sind(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a sine of a given value, expressed in degree This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>sinh</td><td><p>sinh(x)<br />sinh(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic sine of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>size</td><td><p>size(U)<br />size(U, m)</p></td><td><p>To return a size of a given matrix. If only the matrix is passed to the function as an argument, the returned value is a 1x2 matrix. The first Element <br />is the number of rows and the second Element is the number of columns.If the second parameter (m) is specified, this function will return the size of an mth dimension of a given matrix as a scalar value. The second argument can be 1 or 2 (1 for the row size and 2 for the column size). For example:<br />U = [1, 2, 3; 4, 5, 6];<br />size(U) is [2, 3]<br />size(U, 1) is 2<br />size(U, 2) is 3</p></td></tr><tr><td>sort</td><td><p>sort(U)<br />sort(U, ‘descend’)</p></td><td><p>To sort the Elements of a given matrix in an ascending or descending order. If the second argument is specified with ‘ascend’ or ‘descend’, the Elements will be in an ascending or descending order respectively. If this function is called without a second argument, the Elements will be sorted in an ascending order.<br />U is a row or column matrix: <br />sort(U) and sort(U, ascend) sort all Elements in the given matrix. <br /><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">U is a 2-D matrix: sort(U) and sort(U, ascend) sort Elements in each column of the given matrix.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>ssqrt</td><td><p>sqrt(x)<br />sqrt(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a correctly rounded positive square root of a double value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>std</td><td><p>std(U)<br />std(U, flag)</p></td><td><p>To return a standard deviation of a given matrix. The ’flag’ argument can be 0 or 1. It specifies the method for calculating the standard deviation. <br />If the flag = 0, the standard deviation is normalized by N-1. If the flag = 1, the standard deviation is normalized by N where N is the number of data. <br />The value of the flag will be zero by default. U is a row or column matrix: <br />std(U) and std(U, flag) returns the standard deviation of all Elements in the given matrix. U is 2-D matrix: std(U) and std(U,flag) returns a row matrix that contains the standard deviation of each column of the given matrix.</p></td></tr><tr><td>str2num</td><td>str2num(s)</td><td>To return a number specified by a given string s.</td></tr><tr><td>sum</td><td>sum(U)</td><td>To return a summation of all Elements in a U matrix.</td></tr><tr><td>tan</td><td><p>tan(x)<br />tan(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a trigonometric tangent of an angle. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>tand</td><td><p>tand(x)<br />tand(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a tangent of a given value expressed in degree.This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>tanh</td><td><p>tanh(x)<br />tanh(c)</p></td><td><p>To return a hyperbolic tangent of a given value. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>toDegrees</td><td><p>toDegrees(x)<br />toDegrees(c)</p></td><td><p>To convert an angle measured in radians to an approximately equivalent angle measured in degrees. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>toRadians</td><td><br /></td><td><p>To convert an angle measured in degrees to an approximately equivalent angle measured in radians. This function operates Element-wise on matrices.</p></td></tr><tr><td>transpose</td><td>transpose(U)</td><td>To return a transposition of a given matrix</td></tr><tr><td>variance</td><td>variance(U)</td><td><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To return a variance of a given matrix. U is a row or column matrix:</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br />variance(U) returns the variance of all Elements in the given matrix. U is a 2-D matrix: variance(U) returns a row matrix that contains the variance of each column of the given matrix.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td>zeros</td><td>zeros(m, n)</td><td>To return an m x n matrix of all 0s.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=269910048 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00364: (2026x) Generate Reusable UI

- page_id: `269910048`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/269910048/2026x+Generate+Reusable+UI
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-10T08:23:36.602+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > (2026x) Reusable UI Mockup
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheGenerate Reusable UIfeature provides an automated mechanism to create a reusable, parameterized User Interface (UI) from an existing UI Frame element. This UI acts as an independent UI prototype that can be integrated into system simulations and composite structure diagrams. The **Generate Reusable UI**command automatically generates a reusable UI from a selected Frame element.

This action is only available for symbols and elements with the **<<Frame>>** stereotype applied.

To generate reusable UI

- Right-click any element with the <<Frame>> stereotype and select Simulation>Generate Reusable UI. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When the**Generate Reusable UI**action is selected, the following steps are performed automatically

1. The selected Frame element is set as a UI.
2. For each UI component (Label, TextField, CheckBox, Slider) within the selected Frame, a Port is created as an inner element of the Frame.
3. Each of the UI components is assigned a Port.

Once the Reusable UI is generated, you can drag and drop it into an**IBD diagram**. This automatically displays the property typed by the Reusable UI, along with its ports. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generated Reusable UI dragged and dropped into an**IBD diagram**.

To connect UI components with system attributes, create a**connector**between the UI port and the system attribute. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Connectors created between the UI port and the system attribute.

When a Reusable UI is used during simulation, the corresponding UI dialog is automatically displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">The<span> </span>Generate Reusable UI<span> </span>feature provides an automated mechanism to create a reusable, parameterized User Interface (UI) from an existing UI Frame element. This UI acts as an independent UI prototype that can be integrated into system simulations and composite structure diagrams. The <strong>Generate Reusable UI </strong>command automatically generates a reusable UI from a selected Frame element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37c1eca8-c855-4d95-8df2-da2c2d8a73a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This action is only available for symbols and elements with the <strong>&lt;&lt;Frame&gt;&gt;</strong> stereotype applied.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;">To generate reusable UI</p><hr /><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="d7c64ab1-c76c-46dd-ba41-ddcd228ad0ee">Right-click any element with the <strong>&lt;&lt;Frame&gt;&gt;</strong> stereotype and select <strong>Simulation&gt;Generate Reusable UI.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate Reusable UI.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">When the<span> </span><strong>Generate Reusable UI</strong><span> </span>action is selected, the following steps are performed automatically</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="fd2536aa-e540-44aa-afe1-b2123456418c">The selected Frame element is set as a UI.</li><li data-uuid="5cb37dbe-4e1a-43c5-9ea8-c5092886abb4">For each UI component (Label, TextField, CheckBox, Slider) within the selected Frame, a<span> </span><strong>Port</strong><span> </span>is created as an inner element of the Frame.</li><li data-uuid="b7ea7193-b8fc-4de3-8d73-825b5466a1f8">Each of the UI components is assigned a Port.<br /><br /></li></ol><p style="text-align: left;">Once the Reusable UI is generated, you can drag and drop it into an<span> </span><strong>IBD diagram</strong>. This automatically displays the property typed by the Reusable UI, along with its ports. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Drag n Drop to IBD.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Generated Reusable UI dragged and dropped into an<span> </span><strong>IBD diagram</strong>.</h6><p style="text-align: left;">To connect UI components with system attributes, create a<span> </span><strong>connector</strong><span> </span>between the UI port and the system attribute.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Connectors added.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Connectors created between the UI port and the system attribute.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b6664cc-13fd-4a6c-b9a9-da375b950631"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a Reusable UI is used during simulation, the corresponding UI dialog is automatically displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625808 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00365: (2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration

- page_id: `255625808`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625808/2026x+Generating+an+HTML+table+from+a+UI+table+Time+series+chart+and+CSV+export+configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:06.412+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > (2026x) HTML UI
- labels: ['generating-html-table', 'html-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

33

##### Generating an HTML table from a UI table

This feature of the Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit enables you to export MagicDraw HTML tables in UI modeling diagrams to HTML5, allowing them to be viewed in a embeddedweb browser. The sample model used on this page, *SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip*, demonstrates how to create a User Interface table in the User Interface Modeling diagram, generate HTML, run the simulation, and view the UI table in HTML5 in your embedded browser.

To generate an HTML table from a UI table component

1. Open the <modeling_tool_install_dir>\samples\simulation\SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip file.
2. Right-click the Model element in the containment tree and select Expert in the Contextual Menu.
3. Select **Create Diagram**>**Other Diagrams** >**User Interface Modeling Diagram**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Drag a UI table component onto the newly created diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. From the Containment tree, drag the**spacecraft**Block present in the **Structure**Packageto the table and using a long drop select**Create UI Prototype.**The table headers will change from**Column 1**and**Column 2**to**Name**and**Value**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] TipIf you drag the **spacecraft** Block to the table quickly, the column name will change automatically, without having to select **Create UI Prototype**.
6. Open the Specification dialog of the UI Table and verify that the Classifier is assigned to the **Represents** property. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] beside the **Value** property and select the values you want to monitor from the tree view in the **Select Nested Properties** dialog and Click **OK.**
8. The selected values will appear in the **Value** property in the UI table Specification window. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Specify the created Table UI as UI of the **Simulation Configuration.**
10. Set the **Show UI AsHTML**property to *true*.
11. Run thespacecraft mass analysis with Table Simulation Configuration. The HTML table will be opened in an embedded browser.

You can edit the**Documentation/Comments**tag from the UI table's Specification window and run the simulation again, it will show the table's descriptions. To learn more refer to the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comment' space='MT']page.

##### Generating an HTML table from a Time series chart and CSV export configuration

An HTML table can also be generated from a Time series chart and CSV export configuration.

To generate an HTML table from either a Time series chart or a CSV export configuration

1. Right-click a Time Series Chart or a CSV Export configuration and choose **Simulation**> **Generate HTML Table**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. This will generate an HTML file, which can be viewed either online or offline. A message stating that the HTML table is generated will be displayed in the Simulation Console pane.

#### Sample model

The model used in the figures on this page is the *SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip*****sample model that comes with your modeling tool.

To open the sample

- Find it in the modeling tool <modeling_tool_install_dir>\samples\simulation\SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

