The sample model used on this page, <em>SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip</em>, demonstrates how to create a User Interface table in the User Interface Modeling diagram, generate HTML, run the simulation, and view the UI table in HTML5 in your <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="93e28d1b-49dc-4f1c-bec5-52c790ee2b9c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">embedded </span></ac:inline-comment-marker> browser.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate an HTML table from a UI table component</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2b0611cf-72f1-435d-ad76-fb685e75e2a5">Open the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_install_dir&gt;\samples\simulation\SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip</em> file.</li><li data-uuid="c2fe3bdd-6a45-4725-8d44-fc328b3d7d64"><p>Right-click the Model element in the containment tree and select Expert in the Contextual Menu.</p></li><li data-uuid="70647896-c29a-469d-ac37-e618a80255b3"><p>Select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Other Diagrams</strong> &gt;<strong> User Interface Modeling Diagram</strong>.</p><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UI Diagram creation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="825f2485-637d-4ab3-9945-4ee6caf743d5"><p>Drag a UI table component onto the newly created diagram.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UItable_component.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="6ff3514e-a650-40fd-978d-aab82faf1389"><p>From the Containment tree, drag the<strong> spacecraft </strong>Block present in the <strong>Structure </strong>Package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span>to the table and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">using a long drop</span> select<strong> Create UI Prototype. </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The table headers will change from </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Column 1</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and</span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Column 2</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> to </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Name</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Value</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="name_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17350348-3f6e-416d-8a92-955b8e903119"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you drag the <strong>spacecraft</strong> Block to the table quickly, the column name will change automatically, without having to select <strong>Create UI Prototype</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="1407b68c-3942-41d3-9dbb-a1da12968b8c"><p>Open the Specification dialog of the UI Table and verify that the Classifier is assigned to the <strong>Represents</strong> property.<br /><br /></p><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification of Table spacecraft.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="65a169fc-8de8-4eaa-b9df-c91bc6a82bd1"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="html-table-as-ui-config-d.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> beside the <strong>Value</strong> property and select the values you want to monitor from the tree view in the <strong>Select Nested Properties</strong> dialog and Click <strong>OK.</strong></p></li><li data-uuid="682c0e01-1f0c-4870-b084-b45e1c31c825"><p>The selected values will appear in the <strong>Value</strong> property in the UI table Specification window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selectValueProperties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="04437b24-13d4-40c1-9b35-e1c9a1acaaa7"><p style="text-align: left;">Specify the created Table UI as UI of the <strong>Simulation Configuration.</strong></p></li><li data-uuid="c3c4e909-90a4-4245-81da-194fa3eca281"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Set the <strong>Show <span>UI As </span>HTML </strong>property to <em>true</em>.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="692cd803-1afe-49e6-a989-4e55a1b0c2f5"><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run the </span><span style="text-decoration: none;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">spacecraft mass analysis with Table Simulation Configuration. The HTML table will be opened in an embedded browser.</span></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b09da16d-693f-4062-b3e5-3ff17bd58c40"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can edit the </span><strong>Documentation/Comments </strong><span>tag from the UI table's Specification window and run the simulation again, it will show the table's descriptions. To learn more refer to the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Comment" /></ac:link><span> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Generating an HTML table from a Time series chart and CSV export configuration</h3><p>An HTML table can also be generated from a Time series chart and CSV export configuration.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate an HTML table from either a Time series chart or a CSV export configuration</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cf58adcd-a652-4d5c-8e84-01aa69437f41"><p>Right-click a Time Series Chart or a CSV Export configuration and choose <strong>Simulation</strong>&gt; <strong>Generate HTML Table</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate HTML Table Command.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating an HTML table from a UI table, Time series chart, and CSV export configuration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li data-uuid="6515f534-280b-4921-a088-8a0069891560">This will generate an HTML file, which can be viewed either online or offline. A message stating that the HTML table is generated will be displayed in the <strong>Simulation Console</strong> pane.</li></ol><h2>Sample model</h2><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <em>SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip</em><strong> </strong>sample model that comes with your modeling tool.</p><p>To open the sample</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="15602a10-9bb3-4756-9077-7666875b708c">Find it in the modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling_tool_install_dir&gt;\samples\simulation\SpacecraftMassRollup.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625147 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00366: (2026x) Generating FMU - Technology preview

- page_id: `255625147`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625147/2026x+Generating+FMU+-+Technology+preview
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:22:01.428+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Server-side simulation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can generate an FMU (Functional Mock-up Unit) for external co-simulation of dynamic models that enables the exchange of simulation results between different simulation tools. The generated FMU exposes the defined input and output parameters of a model, as specified in a Simulation Configuration. The generated FMU runs the server-side simulation of a particular Simulation Configuration using given inputs and returns the outputs after each time step. FMUs can be generated for two platforms: Linux 64-bit and Windows 64-bit.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- You must have the same Java HOME version as the modeling tool’s Java version. To check the Java HOME version, run java --version in the command prompt. To check the modeling tool’s Java version, go to Help > About > Environment tab.
- You must have a simulation server certificate to co-simulate the generated FMU.
- You must use the generated FMU with tools that use the same JAVA version as the modeling tool; otherwise, you will not be able to run the generated FMU.
- You must add the JAR files to the plugin library to generate FMU. To learn more, refer to .

FMU generation uses selected Simulation Configuration properties, and can contain the following defined data:

- Start Time, End Time, and Step Size property values.
- Input Parameters property value: the default values from the defined input parameters will be included in the generated FMU.
- Output Parameters property value: the default values from the defined output parameters will be included in the generated FMU.

To generate an FMU

1. Open a Teamwork Cloud project.
2. Right-click a Simulation Configuration and select Simulation > Generate FMU. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_FMU_Command.png']
3. In the Generate FMU dialog specify the following:
  - In the File Directory box, specify the location where the generated file should be stored.
  - Select the Win64 or Lin64 radio button, depending on the platform on which you will run the generated FMU.
  - Optionally, select the **Include login credentials**check box and specify a server-side simulation server address and login credentials or, token to be stored in the generated FMU file. You can leave the **Include login credentials** check box empty and provide the login credentials or token later as FMU parameters while importing an FMU file. In this case, the credentials will not be stored in the generated FMU file.You can also generate a personal accesstoken instead of a username and password. To learn more about generating a token, refer to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating a personal access token' space='MCS']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click Generate .

In the case of an FMU file generation error, see the detailed error message in the log file.

##### Adding JAR files to the library

An external library is required to generate the FMU files. You must add the *JAR*files to the plugin library folder.

To add the *JAR*files to the plugin library

1. Download the [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu-builder-2.6.1.jar'] and the [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu-framework-2.6.1.jar'] files.
2. Copy the downloaded JAR files to the <modeling tool installation directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\lib path .
3. Restart the modeling tool after copying the *JAR* files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The generated FMU exposes the defined input and output parameters of a model, as specified in a Simulation Configuration. The generated FMU runs the server-side simulation of a particular Simulation Configuration using given inputs and returns the outputs after each time step. FMUs can be generated for two platforms: Linux 64-bit and Windows 64-bit.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e966c364-7df9-46fa-9614-72a8d454d5a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="a13191a8-e7e1-4b22-9e42-1d63405ca02d">You must have the same Java HOME version as the modeling tool’s Java version. You must add the <em>JAR </em>files to the plugin library folder.</p><p>To add the <em>JAR </em>files to the plugin library</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1050398c-29a3-4cd2-8060-a39ff0cd5fbd">Download the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu-builder-2.6.1.jar" /></ac:link><em> </em>and the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu-framework-2.6.1.jar" /></ac:link> files.</li><li data-uuid="57717fe4-fa8a-418e-9fda-e9d15b5ff107">Copy the downloaded <em>JAR </em>files to the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.simulation\lib </em>path<em>.</em></li><li data-uuid="765087e6-9c44-456a-800b-d46fb667bfe7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Restart the modeling tool after copying the <em>JAR</em> files.</span></li></ol>

## PAGE 00369: (2026x) Getting simulation time

- page_id: `255625135`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625135/2026x+Getting+simulation+time
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:25.346+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Action languages > (2026x) Action scripts APIs > (2026x) Manipulating runtime objects using scripts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

##### Accessing the current simulation time

Simulation time is a timestamp that is retrieved from a simulation clock. You can use ALH API to get the simulation time of execution.

```text

```

The following code fragment shows how to get the simulation time of execution through ALH API.

```text

```

Supported time units are nanosecond(ns), microsecond(µs), millisecond(ms), second(s), minute(min), hour(h), day(d), week(w), month(mo), year(y).

##### Accessing a simulation time unit

The simulation time unit is defined in the tag "timeUnit" of an SimulationConfig stereotype. If you do not define the value of this tag, the default unit of time is the millisecond. You can use ALH API to get a simulation time unit of execution.

The following code fragment shows how to get the*TimeUnit*tag of a**SimulationConfig**through ALH API.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bc6051c-b04b-489a-94c8-3444d1b2297c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Accessing the current simulation time</h3><p>Simulation time is a timestamp that is retrieved from a simulation clock. You can use ALH API to get the simulation time of execution.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92a783e2-38fa-4949-8ef3-4a871a07be7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALH.getCurrentTime()
ALH.getCurrentTime("timeUnit")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The following code fragment shows how to get the simulation time of execution through ALH API.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0491714d-caca-4c58-be6e-b5045e9f243b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALH.getCurrentTime();
ALH.getCurrentTime("ms");					//You can use either the name or abbreviation of the time unit.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e19fb120-adc9-4f1c-8fb4-c4dfacded5b1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Supported time units are nanosecond(ns), microsecond(<span style="color: rgb(0,29,53);">µs</span>), millisecond(ms), second(s), minute(min), hour(h), day(d), week(w), month(mo), year(y).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Accessing a simulation time unit</h3><p>The simulation time unit is defined in the tag &quot;timeUnit&quot; of an SimulationConfig stereotype. If you do not define the value of this tag, the default unit of time is the millisecond. You can use ALH API to get a simulation time unit of execution.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The following code fragment shows how to get the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">TimeUnit</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> tag of a </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">SimulationConfig</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> through ALH API.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23f48c62-df1d-424e-ab6f-affcdbd87006"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALH.getTimeUnit();          // millisecond]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625414 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00370: (2026x) Getting started

- page_id: `255625414`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625414/2026x+Getting+started
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:44.259+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['getting-started']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1326481356

1326481358

1326481357

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is a MagicDraw plugin, which provides a unique set of tools supporting the standardized construction, verification, and simulation of computational complete models based on a foundational subset of the UML. It also enables Activity, State Machine and parametric simulation (execution), animation, and debugging in UML and SysML models, including profiles, samples, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key features' space=''].

No Magic is the first in the industry to provide customers with an easy-to-use, standard-based executable UML solution that integrates the semantics of different UML behaviors. The purpose of this Getting Started section is to provide you with an overview of the plugin and a brief explanation of how exactly this plugin works.

1326481355

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Behavior' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e27ed8-6e30-4220-b561-4326d5673454"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1326481356</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c7d2d85-0d82-48a3-ab9e-fe604e9e20f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1326481358</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="471ddb43-26e1-4ded-b9b8-273a4d65d164"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1326481357</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is a MagicDraw plugin, which provides a unique set of tools supporting the standardized construction, verification, and simulation of computational complete models based on a foundational subset of the UML. It also enables Activity, State Machine and parametric simulation (execution), animation, and debugging in UML and SysML models, including profiles, samples, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Key features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[key features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>No Magic is the first in the industry to provide customers with an easy-to-use, standard-based executable UML solution that integrates the semantics of different UML behaviors. The purpose of this Getting Started section is to provide you with an overview of the plugin and a brief explanation of how exactly this plugin works.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ab5e8e-2365-4308-8388-8bc7c8407f0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1326481355</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5ba7d632-fdd9-44aa-9ff8-f3b99fe190e5" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00371: (2026x) Getting the caller of a script

- page_id: `255625124`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625124/2026x+Getting+the+caller+of+a+script

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once a script has been evaluated, you can use the following ALH APIto trace from where the script was called and access the Caller.

```text

```

It will return the Caller element of the script. For example, if a Behavior that runs a script is called from a callBehavior, you will get a callBehavior action as the Caller.

The following code fragment shows how to trace from where the script was called, e.g., from *State1*, through ALH API.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once a script has been evaluated, you can use the following <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ALH API </span>to trace from where the script was called and access the Caller.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfd0ff97-c8ef-4795-a391-39f1283d8240"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALH.getCaller()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>It will return the Caller element of the script. For example, if a Behavior that runs a script is called from a callBehavior, you will get a callBehavior action as the Caller.</p><p>The following code fragment shows how to trace from where the script was called, e.g., from <em>State1</em>, through ALH API.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ade77bb0-678c-4329-8167-6ca76c6cb6cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ALH.getCaller().getName();		// Trace from State1.]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625992 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00373: (2026x) Histogram

- page_id: `255625992`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625992/2026x+Histogram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:19.914+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['histogram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tips

1399917931

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tips

1399917934

1399917930

**On this page**

32

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tips

1399917932

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces built-in support for Monte Carlo analysis, allowing you to manage uncertainties and estimate how random variation of sensitive parameters affects the overall performance of the system being modeled. A Histogram shows the density of the underlying distribution of data and estimates the probability density function of underlying variables. The total area of the Histogram used for probability density is always normalized to 1. A Histogram also allows you to dynamically view updates on those statistical values during the simulation. In addition, you can customize itd properties, including title, plot color, and labels. For more information about using the Histogram in your model, see the *<install_root>\samples\simulation\HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip* sample as a demonstration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Histogram with dynamic statistical results (N, Mean, SD, and OutOfSpec).

#### Properties of the Histogram

The Specification dialog of the Histogram contains the properties you can select and modify to change the display of the Histogram, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The properties of the Histogram in its Specification dialog.

The following table describes the function of each property in the Specification dialog of the Histogram

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Dynamic | Set to true to open the Histogram during multiple executions to show dynamic results. Otherwise, the Histogram is opened when all executions are completed.NoteWhen the Dynamic property is set to true, the animation of the Histogram with dynamic statistical results (N, Mean, SD, and OutOfSpec) is available. |
| Grid X | Set to true to show a vertical grid line. |
| Grid Y | Set to true to show a horizontal grid line. |
| Keep Open After Termination | Set to true to keep the Histogram open after the termination of the simulation. |
| Plot Color | Specify a plot color. |
| Record Plot Data As | Specify the selected format of the file to be saved if the Result File property is specified. |
| Result File | Specify the name of the file to be saved with the results in the file format specified in the Record Plot Data As property. Otherwise, the results are saved in a model element. |
| Title | Specify the title of the Histogram. |
| X Label | Specify the label of the x-axis of the Histogram. |
| Y Label | Specify the label of the y-axis of the Histogram. |
| Number Of Bins | Specify the number of data bins in which the results are distributed. Otherwise, the number of bins is dynamic, depending on the number of samples. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Histogram can be used only as a local user interface, and the **Number Of Runs**property of the SImulation Configuration must be set to more than 1.

#### Using the Histogram

To use the Histogram

1. Create a Histogram element to represent a Classifier, e.g., Hinge Analysis .
2. In the Specification window of the Histogram, specify the Value property and the properties whose values will be monitored in the Histogram. These properties must be the members of the Classifier represented by the Histogram, e.g., hinge.clearance .
3. Specify other Histogram properties if necessary (see the property table).
4. Drag the Histogram to a Simulation Configuration.
5. Make sure that the value of the UI property of the Simulation Configuration is set to the created Histogram.
6. Run the Simulation Configuration.

#### TIP: Tips

Tips

You can export the Histogram data as a CSV file by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon at the top-left of the Histogram pane. The Histogram is then exported as a CSV file, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Histogram is exported as a CSV file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="084d5af1-fc21-438d-bebe-f54aa7d38679"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1399917931</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79c734fc-0775-4fdd-b5c0-7a1ab771a4fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1399917934</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01314ade-99e3-4d7d-bb81-70122b01627c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1399917930</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b4ac3c2-e0d1-461b-8e51-c38b9b5bf5ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dd7c85a-a4e3-429e-aeaa-9322507933a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1399917932</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces built-in support for Monte Carlo analysis, allowing you to manage uncertainties and estimate how random variation of sensitive parameters affects the overall performance of the system being modeled. A Histogram shows the density of the underlying distribution of data and estimates the probability density function of underlying variables. The total area of the Histogram used for probability density is always normalized to 1. A Histogram also allows you to dynamically view updates on those statistical values during the simulation. In addition, you can customize itd properties, including title, plot color, and labels. For more information about using the Histogram in your model, see the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;\samples\simulation\HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip</em> sample as a demonstration.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Histogram - Monte Carlo.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Histogram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Histogram with dynamic statistical results (N, Mean, SD, and OutOfSpec).</h6><h2>Properties of the Histogram</h2><p>The Specification dialog of the Histogram contains the properties you can select and modify to change the display of the Histogram, as shown below.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Histogram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Histogram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The properties of the Histogram in its Specification dialog.</h6><p>The following table describes the function of each property in the Specification dialog of the Histogram</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 99.9444%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 19.9637%;" /><col style="width: 80.0678%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Dynamic</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set to <em>true</em> to open the Histogram during multiple executions to show dynamic results. Otherwise, the Histogram is opened when all executions are completed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8747645-0661-4d08-9189-a3674621603d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the <strong>Dynamic</strong> property is set to <strong>true</strong>, the animation of the Histogram with dynamic statistical results (<strong>N</strong>, <strong>Mean</strong>, <strong>SD</strong>, and <strong>OutOfSpec</strong>) is available.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Grid X</strong></td><td>Set to <em>true</em> to show a vertical grid line.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Grid Y</strong></td><td>Set to <em>true</em> to show a horizontal grid line.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Keep Open After Termination</strong></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Set to <em>true</em> to keep the Histogram open after the termination of the simulation.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Plot Color</strong></td><td>Specify a plot color.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Record Plot Data As</strong></td><td>Specify the selected format of the file to be saved if the <strong>Result File</strong> property is specified.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Result File</strong></td><td>Specify the name of the file to be saved with the results in the file format specified in the <strong>Record Plot Data As</strong> property. Otherwise, the results are saved in a model element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Title</strong></td><td>Specify the title of the Histogram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>X Label</strong></td><td>Specify the label of the x-axis of the Histogram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Y Label</strong></td><td>Specify the label of the y-axis of the Histogram.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Number Of Bins</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Specify the number of data bins in which the results are distributed. Otherwise, the number of bins is dynamic, depending on the number of samples.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b3b9143-fb7d-45ee-af8a-b385aedbe550"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Histogram can be used only as a local user interface, and the <strong>Number Of Runs </strong>property of the SImulation Configuration must be set to more than 1.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h2>Using the Histogram</h2><p>To use the Histogram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Histogram element to represent a Classifier, e.g., <em>Hinge Analysis</em>.</li><li>In the Specification window of the Histogram, specify the <strong>Value</strong> property and the properties whose values will be monitored in the Histogram. These properties must be the members of the Classifier represented by the Histogram, e.g., <em>hinge.clearance</em>.</li><li>Specify other Histogram properties if necessary (see the property table).</li><li>Drag the Histogram to a Simulation Configuration.</li><li>Make sure that the value of the <strong>UI </strong>property of the Simulation Configuration is set to the created Histogram.</li><li><p>Run the Simulation Configuration.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7a698f7-e5e9-4f58-90db-b0414efb81fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tips</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can export the Histogram data as a CSV file by clicking the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export CSV Button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Histogram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon at the top-left of the Histogram pane. The Histogram is then exported as a CSV file, as shown below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Histogram CSV Exported File.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Histogram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Histogram is exported as a CSV file.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625806 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00374: (2026x) HTML UI

- page_id: `255625806`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625806/2026x+HTML+UI
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:06.038+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit enables you to export, open, and use HTML UI mockups and control model simulations within an embedded browser. To automatically generate the HTML files, you must set the **Show UI As HTML** property to *true*before running a **SimulationConfig.**While the simulation is running, hyperlinks to the generated HTML files are saved in the local project folder and will appear in the **Simulation Console** tab. The corresponding HTML UI is also opened in the embedded browser. You can customize these HTML files at any time and regenerate them using the **Generate HTML** button on the toolbar of the UI modeling diagram.

To learn more about HTML UI, refer to the following pages:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;" data-start="83" data-end="226">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit enables you to export, open, and use HTML UI mockups and control model simulations within an embedded browser. To automatically generate the HTML files, you must set the <strong>Show UI As HTML</strong> property to <em>true </em>before running a <strong>SimulationConfig. </strong>While the simulation is running, hyperlinks to the generated HTML files are saved in the local project folder and will appear in the <strong data-start="497" data-end="519">Simulation Console</strong> tab. The corresponding HTML UI is also opened in the embedded browser. You can customize these HTML files at any time and regenerate them using the <strong data-start="668" data-end="685">Generate HTML</strong> button on the toolbar of the UI modeling diagram.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c60bc3ef-4c83-4b50-861b-62984c68033f">To</ac:inline-comment-marker> learn more about HTML UI, refer to the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ae60b508-c793-42b3-85be-9b01b871dd5a" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625717 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00375: (2026x) ImageSwitcher and ActiveImage

- page_id: `255625717`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625717/2026x+ImageSwitcher+and+ActiveImage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:58.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['image-switcher', 'active-image']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1354113671

1354113673

1354113672

ImageSwitcher is a predefined subtype of UI config. It is a simple, yet flexible and powerful animation tool. You can use ImageSwitcher to represent the state or the enumeration value of a runtime object. To easily create an «ImageSwitcher» element, specify a represented Classifier, and create as many attributes and different States as you wish to see them animate. Each attribute is called an «ActiveImage» and has the following properties

- Image An image that will be used in animation either from browsing the file or dragging the image directly from a web browser.
- activeElement An element that will use an image once it is activated. An active element can be set to represent an active State, Action, or an Enumeration Literal.
- onClick A signal that will be triggered once an image is clicked.

1354113670

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="547ef61c-798b-4794-a4bd-d05cb76f0ed5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1354113671</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="288e016e-2a13-4937-9496-3c2a1ca45f88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1354113673</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c04e0a6-c54c-4006-aea8-84747229d5eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1354113672</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>ImageSwitcher is a predefined subtype of UI config. It is a simple, yet flexible and powerful animation tool. You can use ImageSwitcher to represent the state or the enumeration value of a runtime object. To easily create an «ImageSwitcher» element, specify a represented Classifier, and create as many attributes and different States as you wish to see them animate. Each attribute is called an «ActiveImage» and has the following properties</p><ul><li><strong>Image<br /></strong>An image that will be used in animation either from browsing the file or dragging the image directly from a web browser.</li><li><strong>activeElement</strong><br />An element that will use an image once it is activated. An active element can be set to represent an active State, Action, or an Enumeration Literal.</li><li><strong>onClick</strong><br />A signal that will be triggered once an image is clicked. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2580dc0-cb1d-4853-b4e4-cb6bb58a9eef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1354113670</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8b5270fc-58f5-4f0a-a297-4171960c19f7" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626235 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00376: (2026x) Importing external libraries

- page_id: `255626235`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626235/2026x+Importing+external+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:35.346+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Action languages
- labels: ['importing-external-libraries', 'external-libraries']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you use a scripting language in your model, you may need to call some external libraries to do some specific tasks. Therefore, you must add external libraries to your project before executing it.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only *.jar files are supported as external libraries.

To add an external library to a project

1. On the main menu, click Options and select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. On the left pane, click General > Simulation>External Libraries.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the Select Files and/or Directories dialog. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Select Files and/or Directories dialog, click **Add. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
5. In the Open dialog, select the required file and click **Open**. The path filename will be added to theSelect Filesand/or Directoriesdialog. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Once you click **Open**, a Use Path Variables dialog may appear. The dialog suggests an alternative file path name. Clear the checkbox if you wish not to see the dialog in the future. To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating path variables' space='MT'].
6. Confirm the path file name in the Select Files and/or Directories dialog and click **OK**. Once you click **OK**, a Use Path Variables dialog may appear. The dialog suggests an alternative file path name. Clear the checkbox if you wish not to see the dialog in the future. To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating path variables' space='MT'].
7. Confirm the path file name in the Project Options dialog and click **OK**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you use a scripting language in your model, you may need to call some external libraries to do some specific tasks. Therefore, you must add external libraries to your project before executing it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802c94a2-8f19-4800-b067-a1955d7a5dce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only *.jar files are supported as external libraries.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To add an external library to a project <span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>and select<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. The<span> </span>Project Options<span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>On the left pane, click<span> </span><strong>General</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Simulation&gt;External Libraries.</strong></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Three dot symbol.png" /></ac:image> to open the Select Files and/or Directories dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click Edit.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>In the Select Files and/or Directories dialog, click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Add.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Files and Directories dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></p></li><li><p>In the Open dialog, select the required file and click <strong>Open</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The path filename will be added to the </span>Select Files<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> </span>and/or Directories<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> dialog.</span><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1f4b827-2ba3-48ee-a560-62ad50bbdef9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Once you click <strong>Open</strong>, a Use Path Variables dialog may appear. The dialog suggests an alternative file path name. Clear the checkbox if you wish not to</span> see the dialog in the future. To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Creating path variables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Path variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></li><li>Confirm the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">path file name in the </span>Select Files<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> </span>and/or Directories<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> dialog and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bccb4b8c-ee51-4764-a172-31253dd01d3b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Once you click <strong>OK</strong>, a Use Path Variables dialog may appear. The dialog suggests an alternative file path name. Clear the checkbox if you wish not to</span> see the dialog in the future. To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Creating path variables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Path variables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Confirm the path<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> file name in the Project Options dialog and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /></span></li></ol>
````
