<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626161 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00399: (2026x) Many-to-one binding calculations

- page_id: `255626161`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626161/2026x+Many-to-one+binding+calculations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:32.965+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Value binding
- labels: ['many-to-one-binding-calculations']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Constraint Blocks in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit support many value properties connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification without updating values back to those properties. Any value properties which are not connected to the constraint parameter will not be used as an input. The constraint parameter [*] shows a list of the connected value properties in the **Variables** pane, **Console** pane (Info), and during debugging. The result from the specification of the constraint Block is calculated from the list of value properties. For example:

```text

```

You can also use Add/Remove values with the parameter [*] in the **Variables** pane as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through many value properties.

A constraint Block supports many multiplicities of Part property connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification as in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through multiplicities of Part property.

A constraint Block supports many multiplicities of Participant property of an association Block connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification. The Connector must be set type as the association Block in order to be a member of the input. Also, **{end=...}** must be specified for each Participant. Any participant properties of a Connector property will be synchronized with both the constraint parameter [*] and value properties as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through multiplicities of Participant property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Constraint Blocks in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit support many value properties connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification without updating values back to those properties. Any value properties which are not connected to the constraint parameter will not be used as an input. The constraint parameter [*] shows a list of the connected value properties in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane, <strong>Console</strong> pane (Info), and during debugging. The result from the specification of the constraint Block is calculated from the list of value properties. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="907df08e-f0bb-4aef-a64a-ba1e1ebd20ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[inputs [*] = [2.0;3.0;4.0];
output = sum(inputs);
output = 9.0;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can also use Add/Remove values with the parameter [*] in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane as shown in the following figure.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Many-to-one Binding Supported through Value Properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Many-to-one binding calculations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through many value properties.</h6><p>A constraint Block supports many multiplicities of Part property connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification as in the figure below.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Many-to-one Binding Supported through Multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Many-to-one binding calculations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through multiplicities of Part property.</h6><p>A constraint Block supports many multiplicities of Participant property of an association Block connected to a constraint parameter with multiplicity [*] to perform a specification. The Connector must be set type as the association Block in order to be a member of the input. Also, <strong>{end=...}</strong> must be specified for each Participant. Any participant properties of a Connector property will be synchronized with both the constraint parameter [*] and value properties as shown in the figure below.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Many-to-one Binding Supported through Multiplicities of Participant Property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Many-to-one binding calculations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Many-to-one binding supported by constraint Blocks through multiplicities of Participant property.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626265 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00400: (2026x) Mathematical equation

- page_id: `255626265`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626265/2026x+Mathematical+equation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:36.428+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Evaluating expressions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402879988

402879990

402879989

When solving a constraint that is specified by a mathematical equation, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will find a 'target' by evaluating a 'given'. By default, a target is on the left-hand side of the equation and a given on the right-hand side of an equation.

The following figure shows an example of constraint parameters target and given in a mathematical equation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Evaluating a Mathematical Equation in a SysML Model.

As you can see from the example shown in the preceding figure, the block **Circle** contains a constraint property typed by the constraint block **Circle** **Area**. The constraint block **Circle** **Area** has a constraint that is defined by the mathematical expression **{area =** **3.14159 * (radius** **^** **2)}**. The target and the given in the equation are the constraint parameters '**area**' and '**radius**' respectively. These constraint parameters are bound to the value properties of the block **Circle**. The constraint parameter area of e1 is bound to the value property area of the block **Circle**, and the constraint parameter radius of e1 is bound to the value property radius of the block **Circle**. So, the values of the constraint parameters and the value properties bound together are always equal.

If you simulate the block **Circle**, the parametric evaluator in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create an object **Circle** and its internal values. It will substitute the value of radius of the block object **Circle** to the equation and calculate the value of area. Once it obtains the value of area, parametric evaluator will assign it to the area value property of the object **Circle**. If you change the value of the radius, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically calculate and update the value of the area with the result from evaluating the equation.

You can also define a constraint on a UML class to constrain the values of its properties. The following figure shows a constraint on the UML class **Circle**. The constraint is defined by the same Mathematical expression as in the figure below **{area** **=** **3.14159** *** **(radius** **^** **2)}**. If you simulate the class **Circle**, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the value of radius in the equation. It will then use the value resulting from evaluating the value of radius to update the value of property **area**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Evaluating Mathematical Equation on a UML Class.

402879987

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Evaluating expressions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Logical expression' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f229143-5138-4118-b649-9ddcb675ec8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402879988</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45810f19-0e7a-440c-8eed-6a121588c841"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402879990</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db17be82-b2d5-4bda-91e8-059e715cf533"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402879989</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When solving a constraint that is specified by a mathematical equation, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will find a 'target' by evaluating a 'given'. By default, a target is on the left-hand side of the equation and a given on the right-hand side of an equation. </p><p>The following figure shows an example of constraint parameters target and given in a mathematical equation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Evaluating a Mathematical Equation in a SysML Model" ac:alt="Evaluating a Mathematical Equation in a SysML Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="equationSysml.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Mathematical equation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Evaluating a Mathematical Equation in a SysML Model.</h6><p>As you can see from the example shown in the preceding figure, the block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong> contains a constraint property typed by the constraint block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Area</strong>. The constraint block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Area</strong> has a constraint that is defined by the mathematical expression <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">{area =</strong> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">3.14159 * (radius</strong> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">^</strong> <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">2)}</strong>. The target and the given in the equation are the constraint parameters '<strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">area</strong>' and '<strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">radius</strong>' respectively. These constraint parameters are bound to the value properties of the block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong>. The constraint parameter area of e1 is bound to the value property area of the block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong>, and the constraint parameter radius of e1 is bound to the value property radius of the block <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Circle</strong>. So, the values of the constraint parameters and the value properties bound together are always equal. </p><p>If you simulate the block <strong>Circle</strong>, the parametric evaluator in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will create an object <strong>Circle</strong> and its internal values. It will substitute the value of radius of the block object <strong>Circle</strong> to the equation and calculate the value of area. Once it obtains the value of area, parametric evaluator will assign it to the area value property of the object <strong>Circle</strong>. If you change the value of the radius, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically calculate and update the value of the area with the result from evaluating the equation. </p><p>You can also define a constraint on a UML class to constrain the values of its properties. The following figure shows a constraint on the UML class <strong>Circle</strong>. The constraint is defined by the same Mathematical expression as in the figure below <strong>{area</strong> <strong>=</strong> <strong>3.14159</strong> *** <strong>(radius</strong> <strong>^</strong> <strong>2)}</strong>. If you simulate the class <strong>Circle</strong>, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the value of radius in the equation. It will then use the value resulting from evaluating the value of radius to update the value of property <strong>area</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Evaluating Mathematical Equation on a UML Class" ac:alt="Evaluating Mathematical Equation on a UML Class"><ri:attachment ri:filename="equationUml.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Mathematical equation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Evaluating Mathematical Equation on a UML Class.</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7eae6f3c-1c04-44c0-9baf-5c7f5b40112a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">402879987</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Evaluating expressions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Logical expression" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626069 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00401: (2026x) Message

- page_id: `255626069`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626069/2026x+Message
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:24.695+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Interaction simulation > (2026x) Supported elements in interaction simulation
- labels: ['message']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

379978608

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

379978610

379978607

**On this page**

22

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

379978609

According to the UML specification, a message defines a particular communication between lifelines of an Interaction. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates messages whose message sorts are **asynchSignal**, **synchCall**, **asynchCall**, and **reply**. You can specify a connector for the message. If the role of one connector end is the property specified by a source object of the message, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will send the message along the connector. A target object will be the object at the other end of the connector.

#### Asynchronous Signal Message (asynchSignal)

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates an asynchronous signal message by creating a signal object from a signal specified as a signature of the message. It sends the signal object to a target object asynchronously. The following figure shows that the bank sent the signal **maintain** to the teller using an asynchronous signal message. When the target object (the ATM object specifying the teller) received the signal, the State changed to **Maintained**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the simulation of an asynchronous signal message.

#### Synchronous Call Message (synchCall)

When executing a synchronous call message, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a Behavior (the method of called operation of a target object) from beginning to end before it can carry out the next message. The simulation method is similar to that of the call operation Action with "isSynchronous = true".

You can substitute values for input parameters of the called operation by specifying argument values on a synchronous call message.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the execution of an asynchronous call message.

The preceding figure illustrates the synchronous call messages simulation. A synchronous call message **1** shows the bank object calling the operation **retrieveAccount(accountNumber : String)** of the ledger object. The substitution value for **accountNumber** is the value of **lookupAccountNumber**, which is 0003 in this example. **lookupAccountNumber** is the property of the bank.

The simulation looked for the account object with that particular **accountNumber** and returned it to the bank object with a Reply Message **2** (2 is a Reply Message of 1). The returned account object would be specified as the value of the **buyerAccount** represented by the lifeline **buyerAccount:Account**. The bank object would then call the operation **getBalance()** of the account object with a synchronous call message **3**that caused the balance value to reply to the bank with a Reply Message **4** (4 is a Reply Message of 3). Finally, the bank called itself to print the balance value with the operation **printBalance(balance:Real)**.

#### Asynchronous Call Message (asynchCall)

The simulation of an asynchronous call message is similar to that of a synchronous call message. In this simulation, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a Behavior (a method of a called operation of a target object) on a new thread. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will immediately proceed to the next message once the simulation of the Behavior starts. It will not wait until the simulation of the Behavior completes. This type of simulation is similar to that of a call operation Action with "isSynchronous = false".

#### Reply Message (Reply)

A Reply Message is a Message sent as a Reply to a Call Message (synchronous or asynchronous). If there is an argument value specified in the Reply Message, Simulation will evaluate the argument value.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

A Reply Message must have only one returned argument. If the Reply Message violates the rule, Simulation will print an error message in the [**Console** pane](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Console+pane).

If the argument is not the name of a property, Simulation will verify (compare) the returned value from the called operation with the argument value.

If the result of evaluating the argument is the name of a property owned by a target object, a context object, or a source object, the value returned from the operation will be defined as the value of that property. The Reply Message **2** in the preceding figure has an opaque expression whose body is **buyerAccount** as an argument value.

**buyerAccount** is the property of the **Balance Lookup** Class which is the context of a sequence diagram. Therefore, the returned account object from the operation of the ledger object **retrieveAccount(accountNumber : String)** will be set as a value of the property **buyerAccount** when the Reply Message 2 is simulated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c73bb04a-5561-4451-9644-e453a82732b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">379978608</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="180b827a-1de5-45a8-ba0c-9036160554c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">379978610</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c27e332-8567-4399-b301-a4c7301c4b93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">379978607</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8ecc13d-0079-4fc0-a29d-ce53af4e1a78"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">2</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9077a72e-cd29-4583-8fca-1a0b041c9b4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">379978609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>According to the UML specification, a message defines a particular communication between lifelines of an Interaction. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates messages whose message sorts are <strong>asynchSignal</strong>, <strong>synchCall</strong>, <strong>asynchCall</strong>, and <strong>reply</strong>. You can specify a connector for the message. If the role of one connector end is the property specified by a source object of the message, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will send the message along the connector. A target object will be the object at the other end of the connector. </p><h2>Asynchronous Signal Message (asynchSignal) </h2><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates an asynchronous signal message by creating a signal object from a signal specified as a signature of the message. It sends the signal object to a target object asynchronously. The following figure shows that the bank sent the signal <strong>maintain</strong> to the teller using an asynchronous signal message. When the target object (the ATM object specifying the teller) received the signal, the State changed to <strong>Maintained</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Executing An Asynchronous Signal Message to Send a Signal" ac:alt="Executing An Asynchronous Signal Message to Send a Signal"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exeAsynchroSignalmssg.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Message" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the simulation of an asynchronous signal message.</h6><h2>Synchronous Call Message (synchCall)</h2><p>When executing a synchronous call message, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a Behavior (the method of called operation of a target object) from beginning to end before it can carry out the next message. The simulation method is similar to that of the call operation Action with &quot;isSynchronous = true&quot;. </p><p>You can substitute values for input parameters of the called operation by specifying argument values on a synchronous call message. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="A Simulation of Synchronous Call and Reply Messages" ac:alt="A Simulation of Synchronous Call and Reply Messages"><ri:attachment ri:filename="exeSynchroCallmssg.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Message" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the execution of an asynchronous call message.</h6><p>The preceding figure illustrates the synchronous call messages simulation. A synchronous call message <strong>1</strong> shows the bank object calling the operation <strong>retrieveAccount(accountNumber : String)</strong> of the ledger object. The substitution value for <strong>accountNumber</strong> is the value of <strong>lookupAccountNumber</strong>, which is 0003 in this example. <strong>lookupAccountNumber</strong> is the property of the bank. </p><p>The simulation looked for the account object with that particular <strong>accountNumber</strong> and returned it to the bank object with a Reply Message <strong>2</strong> (2 is a Reply Message of 1). The returned account object would be specified as the value of the <strong>buyerAccount</strong> represented by the lifeline <strong>buyerAccount:Account</strong>. The bank object would then call the operation <strong>getBalance()</strong> of the account object with a synchronous call message <strong>3 </strong>that caused the balance value to reply to the bank with a Reply Message <strong>4</strong> (4 is a Reply Message of 3). Finally, the bank called itself to print the balance value with the operation <strong>printBalance(balance:Real)</strong>. </p><h2>Asynchronous Call Message (asynchCall) </h2><p>The simulation of an asynchronous call message is similar to that of a synchronous call message. In this simulation, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a Behavior (a method of a called operation of a target object) on a new thread. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will immediately proceed to the next message once the simulation of the Behavior starts. It will not wait until the simulation of the Behavior completes. This type of simulation is similar to that of a call operation Action with &quot;isSynchronous = false&quot;. </p><h2>Reply Message (Reply) </h2><p>A Reply Message is a Message sent as a Reply to a Call Message (synchronous or asynchronous). If there is an argument value specified in the Reply Message, Simulation will evaluate the argument value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55a1c8d1-e7e9-4c29-9e2e-a1b3d2a33bef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Reply Message must have only one returned argument. If the Reply Message violates the rule, Simulation will print an error message in the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Console+pane"><strong>Console</strong> pane</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If the argument is not the name of a property, Simulation will verify (compare) the returned value from the called operation with the argument value.</p><p>If the result of evaluating the argument is the name of a property owned by a target object, a context object, or a source object, the value returned from the operation will be defined as the value of that property. The Reply Message <strong>2</strong> in the preceding figure has an opaque expression whose body is <strong>buyerAccount</strong> as an argument value.</p><p><strong>buyerAccount</strong> is the property of the <strong>Balance Lookup</strong> Class which is the context of a sequence diagram. Therefore, the returned account object from the operation of the ledger object <strong>retrieveAccount(accountNumber : String)</strong> will be set as a value of the property <strong>buyerAccount</strong> when the Reply Message 2 is simulated. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625202 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00402: (2026x) Model simulation

- page_id: `255625202`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625202/2026x+Model+simulation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-07T15:28:40.404+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['model-simulation', 'simulating-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to run elements simulation in a MagicDraw project. Elements that can be simulated are those that are supported by the simulation engines in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit. Any number of simulation engines can be implemented as separate plugins and registered to Simulation Framework as the engines for some particular types of models.

The following table shows a list of simulation engines and their supported elements that come with Cameo Simulation Toolkit

| Simulation engine | Supported elements |
| --- | --- |
| Activity engine | Activities.Activity diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is an Activity.InstanceSpecifications of a class whose classifier behavior is an Activity. |
| State Machine engine | State Machines.State Machine diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is a State Machine.InstanceSpecifications of a Class whose classifier behavior is a State Machine. |
| Interaction engine | Interactions.Sequence diagrams.Classes whose classifier behavior is an Interaction.InstanceSpecification of a Class whose classifier behavior is an Interaction. |
| Parametric engine | Blocks that contain properties that are bound to other properties (or constraint parameters) with connected binding connectors.SysML Parametric diagrams.InstanceSpecifications of a Block containing properties that are bound to other properties (or constraint parameters) with connected binding connectors. |

To run a simulation, do one of the following

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Elements simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Running a Simulation Configuration' space=''] including setting a target element to be simulated by the Simulation Configuration and run the model from the Simulation Configuration.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity simulation engine' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) State Machine simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interaction simulation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Parametric evaluator' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626026 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00403: (2026x) Model-based clock

- page_id: `255626026`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626026/2026x+Model-based+clock
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:24.606+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling > (2026x) Simulation time and simulation clock
- labels: ['model-based-clock']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1350049295

1350049298

1350049296

Occasionally, you may want to have full control over the amount of simulation time to simulate your model. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to use a value of any selected property to determine the simulation time. With this option, you can control the value of the simulation time with your model. The model that enables you to use the runtime value as the simulation time is called "model-based clock".

Using the model-based clock requires you to specify the property, whose value will be used as the simulation time, as the value of a **timeValue**tag definition of a Simulation Config. You can specify the unit value for the simulation time through the **timeUnit** tag definition of the Simulation Configuration. If the **timeUnit** is unspecified, the millisecond will be used by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue setting for a model-based Clock.

When you select to use a model-based clock, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore the clock ratio. The following are the constraints of the model-based clock

- There should be only one object of a classifier whose property is specified as timeValue. In the preceding figure, the property time of the block Clock has been set as the timeValue of the System Execution , which is a Simulation Config for simulating the System block. There should be only one Clock object in each simulation.
- You should not use a time event to update simulation time in a clock model. Because, it obtains simulation time from the model-based clock. In addition, a time event will be fired at a specific time or duration, and simulation time cannot be updated unless it is fired. Therefore, if you use a time event, simulation time will not be updated at all.

1350049294

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Internal simulation clock' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to use a value of any selected property to determine the simulation time. With this option, you can control the value of the simulation time with your model. The model that enables you to use the runtime value as the simulation time is called &quot;model-based clock&quot;. </p><p>Using the model-based clock requires you to specify the property, whose value will be used as the simulation time, as the value of a <strong>timeValue </strong>tag definition of a Simulation Config. You can specify the unit value for the simulation time through the <strong>timeUnit</strong> tag definition of the Simulation Configuration. If the <strong>timeUnit</strong> is unspecified, the millisecond will be used by default. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue Setting for a Model-based Clock" ac:alt="The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue Setting for a Model-based Clock"><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelbasedclock.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model-based clock" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Simulation Configuration with timeUnit and timeValue setting for a model-based Clock.</h6><p>When you select to use a model-based clock, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore the clock ratio. The following are the constraints of the model-based clock</p><ul><li><span>There should be only one object of a classifier whose property is specified as timeValue. In the preceding figure, the property time of the block </span><strong>Clock</strong><span> has been set as the timeValue of the </span><strong>System </strong><strong>Execution</strong><span>, which is a Simulation Config for simulating the System block. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625096 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00404: (2026x) Monte Carlo simulation

- page_id: `255625096`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625096/2026x+Monte+Carlo+simulation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:30.327+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) Tutorial > (2026x) Analysis pattern
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2145655572

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2145655574

INLINE

2145655571

INLINE

**On this page**

33

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2145655573

INLINE

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces built-in support for Monte Carlo analysis, a technique that involves using random numbers and probability to solve problems. You can manage uncertainties and estimate how random parameters affect the overall performance of the system being modeled. Please refer to the [ATTACHMENT filename='HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip'] sample model on the welcome screen as the feature demonstration with the following steps.

##### Creating a system model

1. Transform the stochastic model: D > A+B+C (random components) to the deterministic model: D-(A+B+C) > 0 .
2. Create the system model and Parts with a Block Definition diagram with required value properties, e.g., Blocks Hinge , AB , C , and D .
3. Create constraint Blocks with parameters and constraint specification according to the requirement, e.g., Clearance_equation , R1 , and R2 .
4. Create a Requirement with a satisfy Relation to the value property to keep the result for the constraint, e.g., Hinge Clearance and Unassemblable .
5. Apply a «distribution» Type to get a set of random inputs of the value properties of the Parts based on Requirements, e.g., «uniform» with max and min and «normal» with mean and standardDeviation . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Create a parametric diagram in the system to bind the value properties to the parameters of the constraint Block, e.g., Clearance equation . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Inheriting the Hinge Analysis Block from the MonteCarloAnalysis Block

1. Create another Block Definition diagram and include an analysis context definition by dragging the MonteCarloAnalysis Block from MD Customization for SysML::analysis patterns into the newly created Block Definition diagram, e.g., AnalysisDefinition .
2. Create and inherit a new Block from the Block Definition diagram created in Step 1 as an analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis , to get OutOfSpec , N , Mean , and Deviation properties. The inheritance can be done by creating a generalization Relation from the MonteCarloAnalysis Block to the system model Block (from the section).
3. Create an association Relation, e.g., hinge , to make the Hinge system model block part of the analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a parametric diagram and binding values

1. To specify a parameter as the target of Monte Carlo analysis, create a parametric diagram in the analysis block to bind properties and constraints. The statistical value will be recorded in Mean as a result of the termination of the analysis loop, e.g., clearance .
2. Connect constraints from Requirements to Mean for the Requirement verification and to OutOfSpec for the percentage of the samples whose Mean value violates any of the attached constraints or Requirements, e.g., R1 and R2 . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a Simulation Configuration diagram and configuring other settings

1. Create a Simulation Configuration diagram, add a SimulationConfig to the newly created diagram, and set the following tags:
  - executionTarget : the analysis block, e.g., Hinge Analysis Block .
  - numberOfRuns : the number of runs, e.g., 5000 .
  - resultLocation : an Instance table, e.g., Analysis results .
  - silent : true for the optimum performance of the simulation.
  - *name* (optional): e.g., *Monte Carlo Analysis*. NoteFor a model that has Behaviors (Classifier Behavior and/or Part Property with Behaviors), see the *autoStart* tag in [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype).If *numberOfRuns* of any table (*executionTarget*) is more than 1, it will be ignored in the Monte Carlo simulation. The table, however, will be run only once.If *silent* is set to false, the simulation will run with animation and idle time for each iteration, which is not practical for the Monte Carlo simulation.
2. Drag a *[Histogram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Histogram)* control from the Simulation toolbar to the Simulation Configuration diagram. You can use the histogram as a local user interface by setting the following tags:
  - represents : the analysis Block, e.g., Hinge Analysis .
  - value : the monitored value property, e.g., clearance .
  - dynamic : true for viewing dynamically updated statistical values ( false will open the histogram at the end of execution).
  - name (optional) : e.g., Histogram .
3. Record generated value properties of every iteration using the [CSV Export](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) CSV+Export) control by setting the following tags:
  - represents : the system model Block, e.g., Hinge .
  - value : related value properties, e.g., a.width, b.width, or clearance .
  - fileName : the exported file name, e.g., clearance.csv .
  - name (optional): e.g., clearance .
4. Drag the Histogram and CSV Export controls to the «SimulationConfig». UI and executionListeners tags will be updated with the names of the Histogram and CSV Export controls accordingly, e.g., Histogram and clearance . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Running SimulationConfig and reviewing results

1. Run the SimulationConfig from the previous section, e.g., Monte Carlo Analysis .
2. During the simulation, the histogram will dynamically show the estimated distribution of the values of the analysis context definition at the top right, e.g., hinge.clearance, N, Mean, SD, and OutOfSpec .
3. The simulation progress bar will be shown with the number of iterations and time elapsed. You can click Cancel to terminate the simulation, and the analysis result will be saved at the terminated iteration. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. The summary result is recorded in the Instance table along with « VerificationStatus » between value property and constraint, e.g., Mean-R1 and OutOfSpec-R2 . You can also see the details of both failed and passed constraints in the tooltip when hovering the mouse over any highlighted values. [ATTACHMENT filename='analysis_results_in_instance_table.png']
5. Sampling results of value properties from applied [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Distribution Extensions' space=''] are exported to the CSV file in the same location as the project. The file can be accessed through the link, e.g., clearance.csv , in the Console pane. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68f1084c-0116-4991-ad4a-cd72c4989f6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2145655572</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae500e7e-f6de-49c0-95dd-09494d86a590"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2145655574</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4229b8ab-1439-47bc-9ab4-bbe91e32803a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2145655571</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="688085a0-3ccf-4141-8356-244892049089"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9cafe3-1f01-4e56-a167-202cdea3627f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2145655573</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit introduces built-in support for Monte Carlo analysis, a technique that involves using random numbers and probability to solve problems. You can manage uncertainties and estimate how random parameters affect the overall performance of the system being modeled. Please refer to the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="HingeMonteCarloAnalysis.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2021xR1" ri:content-title="Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[HingeMonteCarloAnalysis]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> sample model on the welcome screen as the feature demonstration with the following steps.</p><h3>Creating a system model</h3><ol><li>Transform the stochastic model: <em>D &gt; A+B+C</em> (random components) to the deterministic model: <em>D-(A+B+C) &gt; 0</em>.</li><li>Create the system model and Parts with a Block Definition diagram with required value properties, e.g., Blocks <em>Hinge</em>,<em> AB</em>,<em> C</em>,<em> </em>and<em> D</em>.</li><li>Create constraint Blocks with parameters and constraint specification according to the requirement, e.g., <em>Clearance_equation</em>,<em> R1</em>,<em> </em>and<em> R2</em>.</li><li>Create a Requirement with a <em>satisfy</em> Relation to the value property to keep the result for the constraint, e.g., <em>Hinge Clearance </em>and<em> Unassemblable</em>.</li><li>Apply a «distribution» Type to get a set of random inputs of the value properties of the Parts based on Requirements, e.g., «uniform» with <em>max</em> and <em>min</em> and «normal» with <em>mean</em> and <em>standardDeviation</em>.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-1.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Create a parametric diagram in the system to bind the value properties to the parameters of the constraint Block, e.g., <em>Clearance equation</em>.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><br />Inheriting the Hinge Analysis Block from the MonteCarloAnalysis Block</h3><ol><li>Create another Block Definition diagram and include an analysis context definition by dragging the <em>MonteCarloAnalysis</em> Block from <em>MD Customization for SysML::analysis patterns</em> into the newly created Block Definition diagram, e.g., <em>AnalysisDefinition</em>.</li><li>Create and inherit a new Block from the Block Definition diagram created in Step 1 as an analysis Block, e.g., <em>Hinge Analysis</em>, to get <em>OutOfSpec</em>,<em> N</em>,<em> Mean</em>, and <em>Deviation </em>properties. The inheritance can be done by creating a generalization Relation from the <em>MonteCarloAnalysis</em> Block to the system model Block (from the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creatingasystemmodel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a system model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section).</li><li>Create an association Relation, e.g., <em>hinge</em>, to make the <em>Hinge</em> system model block part of the analysis Block, e.g., <em>Hinge Analysis</em>.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-3.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><br />Creating a parametric diagram and binding values</h3><ol><li>To specify a parameter as the target of Monte Carlo analysis, create a parametric diagram in the analysis block to bind properties and constraints. The statistical value will be recorded in <em>Mean</em> as a result of the termination of the analysis loop, e.g., <em>clearance</em>.</li><li>Connect constraints from Requirements to <em>Mean</em> for the Requirement verification and to <em>OutOfSpec</em> for the percentage of the samples whose <em>Mean</em> value violates any of the attached constraints or Requirements, e.g., <em>R1</em> and <em>R2</em>.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-4.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><br />Creating a Simulation Configuration diagram and configuring other settings</h3><ol><li>Create a Simulation Configuration diagram, add a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a> to the newly created diagram, and set the following tags:<br /><ul><li><em>executionTarget</em>: the analysis block, e.g., <em>Hinge Analysis Block</em>.</li><li><em>numberOfRuns</em>: the number of runs, e.g., <em>5000</em>.</li><li><em>resultLocation</em>: an Instance table, e.g., <em>Analysis results</em>.</li><li><em>silent</em>: <strong>true</strong> for the optimum performance of the simulation.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><em>name</em> (optional): e.g., <em>Monte Carlo Analysis</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a639dec-c9c7-42a7-961a-488c112eba6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For a model that has Behaviors (Classifier Behavior and/or Part Property with Behaviors),</span> see the <em>autoStart</em> tag in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a>.</li><li>If <em>numberOfRuns</em> of any table (<em>executionTarget</em>) is more than 1, it will be ignored in the Monte Carlo simulation. The table, however, will be run only once.</li><li>If <em>silent</em> is set to false, the simulation will run with animation and idle time for each iteration, which is not practical for the Monte Carlo simulation.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>Drag a <em><em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Histogram">Histogram</a></em></em> control from the <strong>Simulation</strong> toolbar to the Simulation Configuration diagram. You can use the histogram as a local user interface by setting the following tags:<br /><ul><li><em>represents</em>: the analysis Block, e.g., <em>Hinge Analysis</em>.</li><li><em>value</em>: the monitored value property, e.g., <em>clearance</em>.</li><li><em>dynamic</em>: <strong>true</strong> for viewing dynamically updated statistical values (<strong>false</strong> will open the histogram at the end of execution).</li><li><em>name</em> (optional)<em>:</em> e.g., <em>Histogram</em>.</li></ul></li><li>Record generated value properties of every iteration using the <em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) CSV+Export">CSV Export</a></em> control by setting the following tags:<br /><ul><li><em>represents</em>: the system model Block, e.g., <em>Hinge</em>.</li><li><em>value</em>: related value properties, e.g., <em>a.width, b.width,</em> or <em>clearance</em>.</li><li><em>fileName</em>: the exported file name, e.g., <em>clearance.csv</em>.</li><li><em>name</em> (optional): e.g.,<em> clearance</em>.</li></ul></li><li>Drag the <em>Histogram</em> and <em>CSV Export</em> controls to the «SimulationConfig». <em>UI</em> and <em>executionListeners</em> tags will be updated with the names of the <em>Histogram</em> and <em>CSV Export</em> controls accordingly, e.g., <em>Histogram</em> and <em>clearance</em>.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-5.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><br />Running SimulationConfig and reviewing results</h3><ol><li>Run the SimulationConfig from the previous section, e.g.,<em> Monte Carlo Analysis</em>.</li><li>During the simulation, the histogram will dynamically show the estimated distribution of the values of the analysis context definition at the top right, e.g., <em>hinge.clearance, N, Mean, SD,</em> and <em>OutOfSpec</em>.</li><li>The simulation progress bar will be shown with the number of iterations and time elapsed. You can click <strong>Cancel</strong> to terminate the simulation, and the analysis result will be saved at the terminated iteration.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li>The summary result is recorded in the Instance table along with «<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Recording+verification+status+of+runtime+values">VerificationStatus</a>» between value property and constraint, e.g., <em>Mean-R1</em> and <em>OutOfSpec-R2</em>. You can also see the details of both failed and passed constraints in the tooltip when hovering the mouse over any highlighted values.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="analysis_results_in_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Sampling results of value properties from applied <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Distribution Extensions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[distributions stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are exported to the CSV file in the same location as the project. The file can be accessed through the link, e.g., <em><u>clearance.csv</u></em>, in the <strong>Console</strong> pane.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="7204-8.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Monte Carlo simulation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624739 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00405: (2026x) Nested Connector end

- page_id: `255624739`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624739/2026x+Nested+Connector+end
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:41:58.201+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['nested-connector-end']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the ends of a Connector that connects properties are stereotyped with «NestedConnectorEnd», Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the information from the propertyPath of the nested Connector end to find the right objects that specify the properties at both ends of the Connector. Therefore, you can send a Signal along the Connector to a deep nesting object. You can also use the binding Connector to bind objects that specify the properties at different nesting levels.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the ends of a Connector that connects properties are stereotyped with «NestedConnectorEnd», Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the information from the propertyPath of the nested Connector end to find the right objects that specify the properties at both ends of the Connector. Therefore, you can send a Signal along the Connector to a deep nesting object. You can also use the binding Connector to bind objects that specify the properties at different nesting levels. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626007 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00406: (2026x) Nested property selection for configurations

- page_id: `255626007`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626007/2026x+Nested+property+selection+for+configurations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:21.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['nested-properties', 'selecting-nested-properties-for-configurations']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit, from version 18.4, includes support for nested property selection for configurations, such as Time series charts, Timelines, Sequence diagram generator, and CSV export.

The Specification window for the aforementioned items will have the following two properties

- **Represents** Specifies the Classifier represented by the Configuration.
- Value The Value property takes nested property values.

The **Select Nested Properties** dialog will open if you click the button beside the Value property from the Specification window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Select Nested Properties dialog.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

Before the **Value** property can be specified, the **Represents** property has to be assigned a value first.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit, from version 18.4, includes support for nested property selection for configurations, such as Time series charts, Timelines</span><span style="color: black;">, Sequence diagram generator, and CSV export.</span></p><p class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;">The Specification window for the aforementioned items will have the following two properties<br /></span></p><ul><li class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;"><strong>Represents<br /></strong></span><span>Specifies the Classifier represented by the Configuration.</span></li><li class="Textbody"><strong style="color: black;line-height: 1.42857;">Value</strong><span style="color: black;"> <br />The Value property takes nested property values.</span></li></ul><p class="Textbody"><span style="color: black;">The <strong>Select Nested Properties</strong> dialog will open if you click the button beside the Value property from the Specification window.  <br /> </span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: black;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Select Nested Properties Dialog" ac:alt="Select Nested Properties Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested-properties-dialog-a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Nested property selection for configurations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Select Nested Properties dialog.</span></span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e351f37c-e660-4f44-af9a-ecb849208f4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Before the <strong>Value</strong> property can be specified, the <strong>Represents</strong> property has to be assigned a value first.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625857 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00407: (2026x) Nested UI Configuration stereotype

- page_id: `255625857`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625857/2026x+Nested+UI+Configuration+stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:10.484+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['nested-ui-configuration-stereotype']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1422162079

1422162082

1422162080

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides a «NestedUIConfig» stereotype to create a complex UI mockup, which consists of multiple UI Configs. This stereotype contains two tag definitions: (i) feature and (ii) config. The feature tag is mandatory. It specifies which Property (part) of the context it will represent. The Config tag specifies which existing UI configuration will be used and displayed as the UI of the system part, which is represented by the Property specified in the feature tag. A Nested UI Configuration can also show images of UI that is defined as the tagged value of Config tag definition, such as Panel, activeImage, and Time series chart.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Images of Nested User Interface Configuration.

You can use a NestedUIConfig stereotype to either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Representing parts of a simulation context' space=''] (This part can nest components that represent the nested properties) or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Representing parts using existing UI Configurations' space='']. The NestedUIConfig stereotype can be applied with the UI Panel or UI Group Box. When it is applied and its tag definitions are set, it can be represented as a part of its owner component.

1422162078

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d268bbb-7cad-42d0-b1a8-52eeb434b94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422162079</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81f732fa-cd41-4044-929e-ddef9586e286"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422162082</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60aa8b9d-2aa8-4737-9f0e-b51d8ce12797"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422162080</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides a «NestedUIConfig» stereotype to create a complex UI mockup, which consists of multiple UI Configs. This stereotype contains two tag definitions: (i) feature and (ii) config. The feature tag is mandatory. It specifies which Property (part) of the context it will represent. The Config tag specifies which existing UI configuration will be used and displayed as the UI of the system part, which is represented by the Property specified in the feature tag. A Nested UI Configuration can also show images of UI that is defined as the tagged value of Config tag definition, such as Panel, activeImage, and Time series chart.</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Images of Nested User Interface Configuration" ac:alt="Images of Nested User Interface Configuration"><ri:attachment ri:filename="NestedConfigShowingImages2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Nested UI Configuration stereotype" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Images of Nested User Interface Configuration.</h6><p>You can use a NestedUIConfig stereotype to either <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Representing parts of a simulation context" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[represent a part of a simulation context]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (This part can nest components that represent the nested properties) or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Representing parts using existing UI Configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[representing a part using existing UI Configurations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The NestedUIConfig stereotype can be applied with the UI Panel or UI Group Box. When it is applied and its tag definitions are set, it can be represented as a part of its owner component.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3d3f885-d7ea-4cc0-8051-eab8d2d06aa6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1422162078</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b644387e-6f6b-4afa-aef2-28ab32dc5ebb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625989 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00408: (2026x) Object binding

- page_id: `255625989`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625989/2026x+Object+binding
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:19.362+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Value binding
- labels: ['object-binding']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a binding connector connects properties that is typed by either a class or a block, the runtime value that specifies each property will be the referent of the same block object. Since you can use only a SysML binding connector to bind objects, the SysML plugin is required.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If a binding connector connects properties that is typed by either a class or a block, the runtime value that specifies each property will be the referent of the same block object. Since you can use only a SysML binding connector to bind objects, the SysML plugin is required. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="objectBinding.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Object binding" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Object Binding with a SysML Binding Connector.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626087 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00409: (2026x) Opening a diagram of an executing behavior

- page_id: `255626087`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626087/2026x+Opening+a+diagram+of+an+executing+behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:31.529+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Animation
- labels: ['opening-executing-behavior-diagram', 'diagram-of-executing-behavior']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you simulate a model, the **Sessions** pane will open to display simulation of each element in the model. During the simulation, if you ever need to see what diagram a Behavior being simulated has, you can double-click the session in the **Sessions** pane of the **Simulation** window. The following figure shows the **Sessions** pane when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is executing the **Blackjack** sample (*Blackjack.mdzip*). If you double-click the Behaviors outlined in red, a new tab will open to display the corresponding diagram.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Sessions tab of the Simulation window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you simulate a model, the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane will open to display simulation of each element in the model. During the simulation, if you ever need to see what diagram a Behavior being simulated has, you can double-click the session in the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane of the <strong>Simulation</strong> window. The following figure shows the <strong>Sessions</strong> pane when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit is executing the <strong>Blackjack</strong> sample (<em>Blackjack.mdzip</em>). If you double-click the Behaviors outlined in red, a new tab will open to display the corresponding diagram. </p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Sessions Tab of the Simulation Window" ac:alt="The Sessions Tab of the Simulation Window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sessionsTab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening a diagram of an executing behavior" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The Sessions tab of the Simulation window.</h6><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626200 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00410: (2026x) Operators

- page_id: `255626200`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626200/2026x+Operators
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:34.179+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Built-in Math
- labels: ['operators']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Important

Important

• **x**and **y** represent numerical values or variables. 
• **m**, **n**, and **p**represent integer values or variables. 
• **a**and **b** represent boolean values or variables. 
• **U**and **V**represent matrices of numerical values. 
• **A**and **B**represent matrices of boolean values.

#### Arithmetic operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| + | Addition | x+yU + V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| - | Subtraction | x-yU + V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| * | Multiplication | x*yU*V (U is an m x n matrix and V is an n x p matrix) |
| / | Division | x/y |
| % | Modulus | m%nU + V (U and V are m x n matrices of integer values).This operator operates element-wise on matrices. |
| ! | Factorial | m! |
| ^ | Power | x^y |
| \ | Left division | x\y is equivalent to (1/x) * yU \ V (U and V are m x n matrices) is equivalent to (1/U) * V |
| .* | Element-wise multiplication | U .* V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| ./ | Element-wise division | U ./ V (U and V are m x n matrices) |
| .\ | Element-wise left division | U .\ V (U and V are m x n matrices) is equivalent to (1/U) .* V |
| .^ | Element-wise power | U .^ V (U and V are m x n matrices) |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An Element-wise operator performs an operation on each pair of Elements, which is in the same location, of the operand matrices.

#### Assignment operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| = | Assignment | x=ya=bU=V |

#### Comparison operators

| Operator | Operator name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| > | Greater | x>yU>V |
| < | Less | x<yU<V |
| >= | Greater or Equal | x>=yU>=V |
| <= | Less of Equal | x<=yU<=V |
| == | Equality | x==ya==bU==V |
| != | Inequality | x!=ya!=bU!=V |

All comparison operators operate Element-wise on matrices in the example as follows

A = [1; 2; 3] 
B = [3; 2; 1]

Then 
A>B is [false; false; true];

#### Boolean operators

| Operator | Operator Name | Syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ! NOTnot | NOT | !ANOT Anot A |
| &ANDand | AND | A&BA AND BA and B |
| \|ORor | OR | A\|BA OR BA or B |
| ^XORxor | XOR (exclusive OR) | A^BA XOR BA xor B |

#### NOTE: Important

Important

All boolean operators operate element-wise on matrices in the example as follows

A = [true; true; false; false]; 
B = [true; false; true; false];

Then 
A&B is [true; false; false; false];

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624932 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00411: (2026x) Parameter sweep

- page_id: `255624932`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624932/2026x+Parameter+sweep
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:11.684+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) Tutorial > (2026x) Analysis pattern > (2026x) Trade study analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

One of the ways of specifying alternatives for trade study analysis is Parameter Sweep. Parameter Sweep generates alternatives automatically based on the specified parameters. After you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Trade study analysis' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Trade study analysis' space=''] for this Block, specify the ranges for design variables using one of four available methods described below.

TradeStudyExamples

install_root

##### Specifying ranges by step

If you specify ranges for design variables by using this method, values of a specific property are generated according to the specified step. There are two possible outcomes:

- If you specify a positive step (step > 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by the specified step until the specified maximum value is reached.
- If you specify a negative step (step < 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified maximum value and then decreased by the specified step until the specified minimum value is reached.

To specify ranges for design variables by step

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, and **Step** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=5, and **Step**=1, the generated values are 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=2.5, and **Step**=-0.4, the generated values are 2.5, 2.1, 1.7, and 1.3.If specified ranges are **Max**=10 and **Step**=4, the generated values are 0, 4, and 8. (If the **Min** property value is not specified, the default value is 0.)

##### Specifying ranges by a number of points

If you specify ranges for design variables by a number of points, the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by a calculated step according to the specified number of points until the specified maximum value is reached.****

To specify ranges for design variables by a number of points

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, and **Number Of Points** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=5, and **Number Of Points**=3, a calculated step is 2 and generated values are 1, 3, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=2.5, and **Number Of Points**=6, a calculated step is 0.3 and generated values are 1, 1.3, 1.6, 1.9, 2.2, and 2.5.If specified ranges are **Min**=-15, **Max**=0, and **Number Of Points**=4, a calculated step is 5 and generated values are -15, -10, -5, and 0.

##### Specifying ranges by a number of points and step

If you specify ranges for design variables by using this method, the values of a specific property are generated according to the specified step and number of points. There are two possible outcomes:

- If you specify a positive step (step > 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified minimum value and then increased by the specified step until the number of generated values reaches the specified number of point or maximum value.
- If you specify a negative step (step < 0), the values of a specific property are generated starting with the specified maximum value and then decreased by the specified step until the number of generated values reaches the specified number of points or minimum value.

To specify ranges for design variables by a number of points and step

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the values of the **Min**, **Max**, **Number Of Points,**and**Step** properties. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] ExamplesIf specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Number Of Points**=5, and **Step**=1, the generated values are 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.If specified ranges are **Min**=1, **Max**=4, **Number Of Points**=3, and **Step**=1.8, the generated values are 1 and 2.8. (The value of 4.6 is excluded because it is greater than the maximum value of 4.)If specified ranges are **Max**=1, **Number Of Points**=3, and **Step**=-0.1, the generated values are 1, 0.9, and 0.8.

##### Specifying ranges by a value list

If you specify ranges for design variables by a value list, a set of specified values is used for a specific property.

To specify ranges for design variables by a value list

1. Open the Specification window of the Bound Reference connected to the relevant property.
2. Specify the value of the **Values** property. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If the Values property is specified together with the Step , N umber Of Points , Min , or Max properties, only the Values property will be used in parameter sweep.
- If the Min property value is not specified, the default value of 0 (zero) is used instead.

##### ****Error handling

- WARN: Since the minimal value of the [value name] design variable is not specified, the default value of 0 is used instead. The warning is shown when the Max property value is not specified in these combinations: [ Min , Max , Step ] or [ Min , Max , Number Of Points ], Step <0. Invalid values shall be excluded from the trade study analysis.
- WARN: The maximal value of the [value name] design variable is not specified. If the Number Of Points property value is 1, then the Min property value is taken as an alternative result (calculating the step is not required). In addition, the warning is shown on the console.
- WARN: Since the Number Of Points value of the [value name] design variable is equal to 1, the minimum value is used instead. The invalid values are excluded from the trade study analysis, and a generic warning (The [value name] design variable is specified incorrectly.) is shown on the console when:
  - The Step property is specified in real numbers (e.g., 1.5; 0.25) but the value is typed as an integer. Exception : the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 5.0).
  - The Step is calculated in real numbers, but the value is typed as an integer (e.g. Min =1, Max =10, Number Of Points =10, calculated Step =0.11). Exception : the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 2.0).
  - Neither the Step nor Number Of Points property value is specified.
  - Step =0 Exception : the warning is not displayed if Number Of Points >0.
  - Number Of Points <=0
  - Min >= Max Exception : the warning is not displayed if Number Of Points =1.
