combinations: [<strong>Min</strong>, <strong>Max</strong>, <strong>Step</strong>] or [<strong>Min</strong>, <strong>Max</strong>, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>], <strong>Step</strong>&lt;0. Invalid values shall be excluded from the trade study analysis.<br /><br /></li><li>WARN: The maximal value of the <ins>[value name]</ins> design variable is not specified.<br />If the <strong>Number Of Points</strong> property value is 1, then the <strong>Min</strong> property value is taken as an alternative result (calculating the step is not required). In addition, the warning is shown on the console.<br /><br /></li><li>WARN: Since the Number Of Points value of the <ins><span class="error">[value name]</span></ins> design variable is equal to 1, the minimum value is used instead.<br />The invalid values are excluded from the trade study analysis, and a generic warning (The <ins><span class="error">[value name]</span></ins> design variable is specified incorrectly.) is shown on the console when:<br /><ul><li>The <strong>Step</strong> property is specified in real numbers (e.g., 1.5; 0.25) but the value is typed as an integer.<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 5.0).</li><li>The <strong>Step</strong> is calculated in real numbers, but the value is typed as an integer (e.g. <strong>Min</strong>=1, <strong>Max</strong>=10, <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=10, calculated <strong>Step</strong>=0.11).<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if there is only a zero after the decimal point (e.g., 1.0; 2.0).</li><li>Neither the <strong>Step</strong> nor <strong>Number Of Points</strong> property value is specified.</li><li><strong>Step</strong>=0<br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if <strong>Number Of Points</strong>&gt;0.</li><li><strong>Number Of Points</strong>&lt;=0</li><li><strong>Min</strong>&gt;=<strong>Max</strong><br /><ins>Exception</ins>: the warning is not displayed if <strong>Number Of Points</strong>=1.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626319 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00412: (2026x) Parametric debugging

- page_id: `255626319`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626319/2026x+Parametric+debugging
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:28.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator
- labels: ['parametric-animation', 'animating-parametrics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Simulation Toolkit allows debugging the Parametric diagram through [Breakpoints](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Breakpoints). It is especially helpful when working on projects with a large number of elements or parametric patterns that are applied recursively. Because the Parametric diagram usually runs at the maximum speed (without delay) regardless of the **Animation Speed** settings (see also [SimulationConfig](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype)), you must set Breakpoints to monitor values.

In the figure below, Simulation pauses at the *margin* breakpoint in the Parametric diagram in which you can debug the model. You can also find the sample model of parametric debugging on the Welcome screen by going to the **Simulation** sample group and selecting **SpacecraftMassRollup**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Debugging parameters of the SpacecraftMassRollup built-in sample.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Simulation Toolkit allows debugging the Parametric diagram through <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Breakpoints">Breakpoints</a>. It is especially helpful when working on projects with a large number of elements or parametric patterns that are applied recursively. Because the Parametric diagram usually runs at the maximum speed (without delay) regardless of the <strong>Animation Speed</strong> settings (see also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype">SimulationConfig</a>), you must set Breakpoints to monitor values.</p><p>In the figure below, Simulation pauses at the <em>margin</em> breakpoint in the Parametric diagram in which you can debug the model. You can also find the sample model of parametric debugging on the Welcome screen by going to the <strong>Simulation</strong> sample group and selecting <strong>SpacecraftMassRollup</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Debugging a Parametric of the Built-in Sample.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Parametric debugging" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Debugging parameters of the SpacecraftMassRollup built-in sample.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625322 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00413: (2026x) Parametric evaluator

- page_id: `255625322`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625322/2026x+Parametric+evaluator
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:41.861+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: ['parametric-evaluator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit comes with the parametric evaluator to help you solve constraint expressions in your model. The parametric evaluator is designed to work with the SysML Parametric Diagram. But, you can also use it to solve constraints on any UML classes. With the parametric evaluator, you can define a mathematical or a logical expression as a constraint on a block or a class to limit the values of its properties. If the expression is an equation, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the expression of the constraint and update the values of the properties with the result of the evaluation. If the expression is a logical expression, the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of the properties.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit comes with the parametric evaluator to help you solve constraint expressions in your model. The parametric evaluator is designed to work with the SysML Parametric Diagram. But, you can also use it to solve constraints on any UML classes. With the parametric evaluator, you can define a mathematical or a logical expression as a constraint on a block or a class to limit the values of its properties. If the expression is an equation, the parametric evaluator will evaluate the expression of the constraint and update the values of the properties with the result of the evaluation. If the expression is a logical expression, the parametric evaluator will use the expression to validate the values of the properties. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624770 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00414: (2026x) Port

- page_id: `255624770`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624770/2026x+Port
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:23.613+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

944122966

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

944122979

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

944122968

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the following SysML ports:

- Flow Port A Port stereotyped with «FlowPort» that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment.
- Full Port A Port stereotyped with «FullPort» that specifies a separate element of an owning Block.
- Proxy Port A Port stereotyped with «ProxyPort» that the value specifying the Proxy Port is the reference of the object that is the target of the Flow.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- A Conjugated Port (isConjugated = True) is indicated with a tilde (∼) in front of the Port type, showing that the property Flow direction of the Port is reversed, from **in** to **out** or from **out** to **in**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']A conjugated Port with a tilde (∼) in front of the Port type.
- For the nested Port, if the parent Port is conjugated, all of the property Flow directions of its children and grandchildren will be reversed.
- A Behavior Port is supported during simulation. If a port is a behavior port (isBehavior = True), it will pass the arriving requests directly to the owning classifier while ignoring all delegation connectors. [ATTACHMENT filename='behavior_port.png']

###### Behavior Port

944122965

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625933 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00415: (2026x) Primitive value binding

- page_id: `255625933`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625933/2026x+Primitive+value+binding
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:15.532+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Parametric evaluator > (2026x) Value binding
- labels: ['primitive-value-binding']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Primitive value binding connects two value properties or properties that are typed by a primitive type so that whenever the value of one property changes, the value of the other property will also change. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit maintains the values of those connected properties to be the same at both ends. Changing the value at one end causes Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to update the value of the property at the other end of the connector. The figures below show the primitive value binding with a SysML binding connector and a UML binding connector respectively.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the execution of blocks. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Simulation console showing the results of a simulation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Primitive value binding connects two value properties or properties that are typed by a primitive type so that whenever the value of one property changes, the value of the other property will also change. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit maintains the values of those connected properties to be the same at both ends. Changing the value at one end causes Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to update the value of the property at the other end of the connector. The figures below show the primitive value binding with a SysML binding connector and a UML binding connector respectively. </p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Primitive Value Binding with a SysML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Primitive Value Binding with a SysML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="primValuebinding_sysml.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Primitive value binding" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the execution of blocks.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:title="Primitive Value Binding with a UML Binding Connector" ac:alt="Primitive Value Binding with a UML Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="primValuebinding_uml.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Primitive value binding" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Simulation console showing the results of a simulation.</h6><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 00416: (2026x) Printing constraint failures in the console

- page_id: `255625361`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625361/2026x+Printing+constraint+failures+in+the+console
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:42.645+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation debugging > (2026x) Simulation console
- labels: ['constraint-failures', 'error-messages-about-constraint-failures']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33730985

33730988

33730986

Most of model simulations involve with evaluating values that are in the model. Evaluation will fail if the model cannot satisfy any of the constraints. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit acknowledges this kind of failure as a constraint failure and thus will print the error message in the **Console** pane. The message will inform you about the unsatisfied constraint(s) including hyperlinks that help you navigate the failed requirements and constraint(s), and the diagram where the simulation stopped.

The following figure shows an example of constraint failures printed on the **Console** pane

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Printing constraint failures in the Console pane.

33730984

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Console pane' space='']

The message will inform you about the unsatisfied constraint(s) including hyperlinks that help you navigate the failed requirements and constraint(s), and the diagram where the simulation stopped. </p><p>The following figure shows an example of constraint failures printed on the <strong>Console</strong> pane</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Printing Constraint Failures in the Console Pane" ac:alt="Printing Constraint Failures in the Console Pane"><ri:attachment ri:filename="110073_ConstraintFail.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Printing constraint failures in the console" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Printing constraint failures in the Console pane.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7efd82d8-72d0-46d1-b2aa-5e4b85e54b65"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33730984</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Console pane" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624753 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00417: (2026x) Probability

- page_id: `255624753`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624753/2026x+Probability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:00.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['probability']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Probability is a stereotype in SysML. You can apply it to outgoing edges of decision nodes and object nodes. When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a decision node or an object nodes whose outgoing edges stereotyped with probability, it will use the probability values collected from all outgoing edges to select one outgoing edge that it will go to.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges.

Normally, the summation of the probability values from all outgoing edges should be 1.0 (100%). If this is not the case, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will scale the probability values with the summation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Probability is a stereotype in SysML. You can apply it to outgoing edges of decision nodes and object nodes. When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates a decision node or an object nodes whose outgoing edges stereotyped with probability, it will use the probability values collected from all outgoing edges to select one outgoing edge that it will go to. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges" ac:alt="Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ProbabilitySupported.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Probability" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Supported Probability Values for Decision Nodes on Outgoing Edges.</h6><p>Normally, the summation of the probability values from all outgoing edges should be 1.0 (100%). If this is not the case, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will scale the probability values with the summation. </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625766 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00418: (2026x) Project options

- page_id: `255625766`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625766/2026x+Project+options
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:26.698+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation Configuration and UI modeling
- labels: ['project-options', 'simulation-project-options']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize a simulation project, e.g., animation, simulation framework, and simulation engines through project options. When you save the project, those project options will also be saved. In addition, you can restore those options to default values with the **Reset to Defaults** button.

To customize project options

1. Open a simulation project.
2. On the main menu, click Options and select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. On the left pane, click General > Simulation .

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The **Options** > **Project****command will be available only if one or more projects are open.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Simulation Project Options dialog.

Groups of the project options are as follows:

- Animation Customize animations of the simulation: colors of annotated elements, auto open diagrams, and silent options. See also customizing animation .
- Simulation Framework Customize general Behaviors of the simulation. See also validation and verification and integration with external Evaluators .
- Sequence Diagram Generator Record the Sequence diagram generator. See also recording simulation as a Sequence diagram .
- fUML Engine Customize Behaviors of Activity simulation. See also Activity simulation engine .
- Parametric Evaluator Customize Behaviors of Parametric simulation. See also integration with external Evaluators and specifying the language for the expression .
- SCXML Engine Customize Behaviors of State Machine simulation. See also completion Events and Transitions and State activation semantics .
- Simulation Script Engine Select JAR file(s) and load them to the script engine.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can customize a simulation project, e.g., animation, simulation framework, and simulation engines through project options. When you save the project, those project options will also be saved. In addition, you can restore those options to default values with the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button.</p><p>To customize project options</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a simulation project.</li><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> and select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>On the left pane, click <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Simulation</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a6fd10-9ccc-4560-af2f-f3757ff64962"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong><em> </em>command will be available only if one or more projects are open.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="All-Project Options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ab6b547-4c31-40e8-98e2-d3cf19ac031c">The Simulation Project Options dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker></h6><p>Groups of the project options are as follows:</p><ul><li><strong>Animation</strong><br />Customize animations of the simulation: colors of annotated elements, auto open diagrams, and silent options. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Activity+simulation+engine">Activity simulation engine</a>.</li><li><strong>Parametric Evaluator</strong><br />Customize Behaviors of Parametric simulation. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625399 space=MMA version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00419: (2026x) Proxy Port

- page_id: `255625399`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625399/2026x+Proxy+Port
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:23.738+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation of SysML models > (2026x) Supported SysML elements > (2026x) Port
- labels: ['proxy-port']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

944183898

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

944183910

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

944183900

A Proxy Port is a Port stereotyped with «ProxyPort». When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates an object that has a proxy Port, the value that specifies the proxy Port will be the reference of the object that is the target of the Flow. A Classifier of the target object must be inherited from an interface Block that types a proxy Port.

If the proxy Port is not connected by a delegation Connector to the internal structure of the owning object, the target will be the object itself. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The target object owning object of the proxy Port.

If the proxy Port is connected by a delegation Connector to the internal structure of the object owning the Port, the target of the Flow will be the object specifying the role at the other end of the delegation Connector. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The target Objects of proxy Ports are visible when the Ports connected by Delegation Connector.

If Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit cannot find the target object, or the classifier of the target object does not inherit from the interface Block that types the proxy Port, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will initialize the Port object directly from its type.

Features defined by Interface Block, e.g., Flow properties, can be displayed in the **Variables** pane (when selecting the **Show Ports** option for the **Variables** pane in the **Project Options** dialog) under Proxy Port nodes during simulation as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Proxy Port nodes (level and valve) show only features defined by «interfaceBlock» (level and valve Flow properties).

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other properties which are not part of the Ports, e.g., *minlevel* and *maxlevel* of *Controller* are not shown because Ports can be only accessed and display what is defined by «interfaceBlock».

944183896

**Related pages**

- Port
- Flow Port
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Full Port' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626230 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00420: (2026x) Reading enumeration literal value

- page_id: `255626230`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626230/2026x+Reading+enumeration+literal+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:35.078+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Action languages
- labels: ['enumeration-literal-value']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can evaluate any expression that contains enumeration literal by name directly, e.g., colour == "Green", but not colour.getName() == Green.

The following figure demonstrates how you can read Enumeration literal value by name directly through the property **Change Expression** colour=="Green" and setting the **guard condition** colour=="Red" instead of colour.getName()=="Green", and colour.getName()=="Red" accordingly in the Specification window of the Transition<>.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reading Enumeration literal value by name directly (without .getName()).

Backward compatibility with earlier version models using getName() is supported through an error message in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Simulation console' space=''] pane. The Script engine will throw one error message, and then stop for Enumeration literal value. If getName() exists in many places, you will have to run the simulation many times and manually fix it one by one.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="Standard">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can evaluate any expression that contains enumeration literal by name directly, e.g., colour == &quot;Green&quot;, but not colour.getName() == Green.</p><p class="Standard">The following figure demonstrates how you can read Enumeration literal value by name directly through the property <strong>Change Expression</strong> colour==&quot;Green&quot; and setting the <strong>guard condition</strong> colour==&quot;Red&quot; instead of colour.getName()==&quot;Green&quot;, and colour.getName()==&quot;Red&quot; accordingly in the Specification window of the Transition&lt;&gt;.</p><p class="Standard"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enum_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reading enumeration literal value" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p><h6 class="Standard" style="text-align: center;">Reading Enumeration literal value by name directly (without .getName()). </h6><p class="Standard">Backward compatibility with earlier version models using getName() is supported through an error message in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f4a377cf-5d6a-4b4f-b1e8-6dbc7ee22ab8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Simulation console" /></ac:link> pane</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The Script engine will throw one error message, and then stop for Enumeration literal value. If getName() exists in many places, you will have to run the simulation many times and manually fix it one by one.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625314 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00421: (2026x) Reading runtime values from an Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `255625314`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625314/2026x+Reading+runtime+values+from+an+Excel+spreadsheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:42:41.574+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Action languages > (2026x) Action scripts APIs > (2026x) Excel Helper APIs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit helps you read fUML runtime values from an Excel file. The following API will read the values of a specific row from a spreadsheet in an Excel file, and then create an fUML ValueList to store the values.

```text

```

The APIs can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

The following API will read the values from multiple rows of the selected spreadsheet in the Excel file, and then create the fUML ValueList to store the values.

```text

```

The APIs can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

If you want to read all values from the selected spreadsheet, you can use the following API:

```text

```

The API can ignore the sheetName and use the first sheet of the selected spreadsheet instead.

```text

```

The mappingElement parameter can be a String or an element for representing a mapping Class name or a mapping Class element.

#### INFO: Information

Information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving an Import Map' space='MT']«ImportMap»can be used as the mappingElement. However, it is strongly recommended that you define the distinctive name of «ImportMap» and the «DiagramTableMapToDataSource» (an Instance table linked to an Excel file) when using to avoid conflict of object mappingElement.

The APIs can ignore the mappingElement then create the runtime object of the file schema element that is matched with filename.

```text

```

The following API can assign a name parameter to check the name of a mappingElement or a sheetName. If the matching mappingElement cannot be found, this API will use the file schema element to create a runtime object and check the sheet name of the Excel file with the name parameter.

```text

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255624875 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00422: (2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram

- page_id: `255624875`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255624875/2026x+Recording+simulation+as+a+Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:24.346+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Interaction simulation
- labels: ['recording-simulation-as-sequence-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The recording capability of the Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to:

- record created objects as CreateMessages connected between Lifelines that represent the object creator and features of the created object respectively.
- record signals as SendMessages connected between Lifelines that represent signal senders and signal receivers respectively. Connectors will be assigned to the messages if signals are sent via ports or connectors.
- record operation calls as CallMessages connected between Lifelines that represent operation callers and operation owners respectively. Connectors will be assigned to messages if operations are called via ports.
- record changes of states and primitive values as StateInvariants on Lifelines that represent features of objects that own the states or the values.

This section demonstrates how to record the signal, state change, operation call, and value change as a sequence diagram during a model simulation. The sample FlashingLight.mdzip, located in the *<*install_root*>/samples/simulation/* directory, will be used throughout this section.

To record signals sent from and to a runtime object and subsequent state/value changes of the related objects as a sequence diagram

1. In the Variables pane, select and right-click a runtime object.
2. Click Create Sequence Diagram on the context menu. An empty sequence diagram will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='Create Sequence Diagram.png']

Whenever you simulate a model (such as Flashing Light as shown in the figure above), Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will:

- create the first Lifeline, which represents the selected runtime object.
- record each signal sent from the selected runtime object as a Message in the sequence diagram.
- record each operation call caused by a call message, a CallOperationAction, or an ALH.callOperation with argument and return value as messages in the sequence diagram.
- record an object that receives a signal and/or an operation call as a Lifeline. Unless it exists in the diagram, the object will be called a 'lifeline object'.
- record each change in the state of a lifeline object as a StateInvariant on the Lifeline, with the changed state symbol.
- record each change in the feature value of a lifeline object as a StateInvariant on the Lifeline. Changes in value are enclosed in constraint brackets, i.e., {a=10}.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

StateInvariants are designated by yellow rounded rectangles. See the following figure for examples.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A sequence diagram depicting call messages running between lifelines.

To see what connector a signal or an operation call is sent through

- Double-click the message or right-click it and select Specification to open the specification window. [ATTACHMENT filename='Specification_Window_Message.png']

To see the values of arguments sent with a signal or an operation call

1. Either double-click a message or right-click it and select Specification to open the specification window.
2. Select Arguments in the tree on the left-hand side of the specification dialog to see the value of each argument.

You can customize recorded messages (signals) and lifelines using **SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig element showing its parameters.

A **SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig** is a stereotype that is inherited from an **ExecutionListener** stereotype. It contains the following six tag definitions:

- Owner An element that owns a generated Interaction element. A generated Sequence diagram will be created under that particular Interaction element. You need to select only the element that can own an Interaction element; otherwise, a model inconsistency will occur.
- Ignored Signals Signals that will be ignored (will not be recorded) during a simulation recording.
- Ignored Lifelines A list of elements (objects) that will be ignored (will not be recorded as lifelines) during a simulation recording. This list takes priority over the value list.
- Recorded Object Path Used to specify a property path to an object that will be recorded by a sequence diagram generator. The path must start from a property owned by either a classifier which is the target of the simulation configuration, or a classifier of an instance specification which is the target of the simulation configuration. The property at each successive position following the first position must be contained in the classifier that types the property at the immediately preceding position.
- Record State Change A boolean option. If true, state changes will be recorded.
- Record Value Change A boolean option. If true, value changes will be recorded.
- Record Timestamp A boolean option. If true, timestamps will be recorded on messages.
- Value A structural feature whose value is represented by the configuration.

You can specify the default values of *recordStateChange*,*recordValueChange*, and *recordTimestamp* through the[**Project** **Options**](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Project+options) dialog. The values in the project options will be used if the tagged values of the sequence diagram generator are not specified. The default values of these project options are **true**,**true**, and **false** (see the following figure). **True** means all values will be recorded by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Options in the Sequence Diagram Generator group in the Project Options dialog.

To customize a Sequence diagram recording

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Model folder (root folder) and select Create Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Simulation Config Diagram in Containment Tree.png']
2. Do one of the following:
  - I n the dialog, expand**Simulation**and select**Simulation Configuration Diagram****.** [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting Simulation Config Diagram 1.png']
  - In the search tab, type the keyword simulation and then select**Simulation Configuration Diagram** . 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. From the Simulation Configuration Diagram palette, drag the Sequence Diagram Generator to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='Drag and Drop.png']
4. Right-click the newly created **SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig** and select **Specification** to open its**Specification**dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Specify the value(s) of the tag definition(s) of the config, e.g., **Name**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] TipYou can specify **Represents** and **Value** to be captured by the **SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig** along with other settings, such as **RecordTimestamp**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Drag the configured SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig to the SimulationConfig element (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SimulationConfig stereotype' space=''] ). The executionListeners tag of the SimulationConfig will be shown with the specified name of the SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig , as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='Final Specified Simulation.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The recording capability of the Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to:</p><ul><li>record created objects as CreateMessages connected between Lifelines that represent the object creator and features of the created object respectively.</li><li>record signals as SendMessages connected between Lifelines that represent signal senders and signal receivers respectively. Connectors will be assigned to the messages if signals are sent via ports or connectors.</li><li>record operation calls as CallMessages connected between Lifelines that represent operation callers and operation owners respectively. Connectors will be assigned to messages if operations are called via ports.</li><li>record changes of states and primitive values as StateInvariants on Lifelines that represent features of objects that own the states or the values. </li></ul><p>This section demonstrates how to record the signal, state change, operation call, and value change as a sequence diagram during a model simulation. The sample FlashingLight.mdzip, located in the <em>&lt;<em style="text-align: left;">install_root</em>&gt;/samples/simulation/</em> directory, will be used throughout this section. <br /> </p><p>To record signals sent from and to a runtime object and subsequent state/value changes of the related objects as a sequence diagram </p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Variables</strong> pane, select and right-click a runtime object.</li><li>Click <strong>Create Sequence Diagram</strong> on the context menu. An empty sequence diagram will be created.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create Sequence Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>Whenever you simulate a model (such as Flashing Light as shown in the figure above), Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will:</p><ul><li>create the first Lifeline, which represents the selected runtime object.</li><li>record each signal sent from the selected runtime object as a Message in the sequence diagram.</li><li>record each operation call caused by a call message, a CallOperationAction, or an ALH.callOperation with argument and return value as messages in the sequence diagram.</li><li>record an object that receives a signal and/or an operation call as a Lifeline. Unless it exists in the diagram, the object will be called a 'lifeline object'. </li><li>record each change in the state of a lifeline object as a StateInvariant on the Lifeline, with the changed state symbol.</li><li>record each change in the feature value of a lifeline object as a StateInvariant on the Lifeline. Changes in value are enclosed in constraint brackets, i.e., {a=10}. </li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c49bee1-aafd-4a17-82fc-e55ab57bd3aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>StateInvariants are designated by yellow rounded rectangles. See the following figure for examples.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Block System.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />A sequence diagram depicting call messages running between lifelines.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></h6><p>To see what connector a signal or an operation call is sent through</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the message or right-click it and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the specification window. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification_Window_Message.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To see the values of arguments sent with a signal or an operation call</p><hr /><ol><li>Either double-click a message or right-click it and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the specification window.</li><li>Select <strong>Arguments</strong> in the tree on the left-hand side of the specification dialog to see the value of each argument. </li></ol><p>You can customize recorded messages (signals) and lifelines using <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="219"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sequence Diagram Generator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />A SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig element showing its parameters.</h6><p>A <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong> is a stereotype that is inherited from an <strong>ExecutionListener</strong> stereotype. It contains the following six tag definitions: </p><ul><li><strong>Owner</strong> <br />An element that owns a generated Interaction element. A generated Sequence diagram will be created under that particular Interaction element. You need to select only the element that can own an Interaction element; otherwise, a model inconsistency will occur.<br /> </li><li><strong>Ignored Signals</strong> <br />Signals that will be ignored (will not be recorded) during a simulation recording.<br /> </li><li><strong>Ignored Lifelines</strong> <br />A list of elements (objects) that will be ignored (will not be recorded as lifelines) during a simulation recording. This list takes priority over the <strong>value</strong> list.<br /><br /></li><li><strong>Recorded Object Path</strong> <br />Used to specify a property path to an object that will be recorded by a sequence diagram generator. The path must start from a property owned by either a classifier which is the target of the simulation configuration, or a classifier of an instance specification which is the target of the simulation configuration. The property at each successive position following the first position must be contained in the classifier that types the property at the immediately preceding position. <br /><br /></li><li><strong>Record State Change</strong> <br />A boolean option. If true, state changes will be recorded. <br /><br /></li><li><strong>Record Value Change</strong> <br />A boolean option. If true, value changes will be recorded. <br /><br /></li><li><strong>Record Timestamp</strong> <br />A boolean option. If true, timestamps will be recorded on messages.<br /><br /></li><li><strong>Value<br /></strong>A structural feature whose value is represented by the configuration.</li></ul><p>You can specify the default values of <em>recordStateChange</em>,<em> recordValueChange</em>, and <em>recordTimestamp</em> through the<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) Project+options"><strong>Project</strong> <strong>Options</strong></a> dialog. The values in the project options will be used if the tagged values of the sequence diagram generator are not specified. The default values of these project options are <strong>true</strong>,<strong> true</strong>, and <strong>false</strong> (see the following figure). <strong>True</strong> means all values will be recorded by default.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sequence Diagram Generator options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Options in the Sequence Diagram Generator group in the Project Options dialog.</h6><p>To customize a Sequence diagram recording <span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the <strong>Model</strong> folder (root folder) and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Simulation Config Diagram in Containment Tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">I</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the dialog, expand<span> </span><strong>Simulation </strong>and select<span> <strong style="text-align: left;">Simulation Configuration Diagram</strong></span><strong>.<br /></strong></span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting Simulation Config Diagram 1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the search tab, type the keyword simulation and then select<span> <strong style="text-align: left;">Simulation Configuration Diagram</strong></span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Search Tab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li></ul></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">From the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">Simulation Configuration Diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad"> palette, drag the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">Sequence Diagram Generator </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">to the diagram pane.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Drag and Drop.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">Right-click the newly created <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong> and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open its </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">Specification</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad"> dialog</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">.<br /><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting Specification Menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2633e203-3b24-46e7-bb1a-f378373a90ad">Specify the value(s) of the tag definition(s) of the config, e.g., <strong>Name</strong>.<br /><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification_Window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b560b0c8-6420-4c5a-a2d6-3c82d6e99cfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify <strong>Represents</strong> and <strong>Value</strong> to be captured by the <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong> along with other settings, such as <strong>RecordTimestamp</strong>.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification_Represents and Value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Drag the configured <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong> to the <strong>SimulationConfig</strong> element (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SimulationConfig stereotype" /></ac:link>). The <strong>executionListeners</strong> tag of the <strong>SimulationConfig</strong> will be shown with the specified name of the <strong>SequenceDiagramGeneratorConfig</strong>, as shown below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Final Specified Simulation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Recording simulation as a Sequence diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255625554 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00423: (2026x) Recording Verification status of runtime values

- page_id: `255625554`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255625554/2026x+Recording+Verification+status+of+runtime+values
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:47:25.988+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Simulation debugging > (2026x) Runtime Value Monitoring
- labels: ['runtime-value-verification-status', 'recording-runtime-value-verification-status']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

46423820

46423823

1996096896

**On this page**

33

46423822

##### Instance Table with simulation results

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit allows you to record the verification status of runtime values. The cells in the Variables pane and in Instance tables are highlighted in green if runtime values meet the constraints during the simulation, and in red - if they do not. You can do the normal recording verification status of runtime values by using the**resultLocation**tag in a[Simulation Configuration](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) SimulationConfig+stereotype). The instances with recorded values in slots are created at the end of the simulation.

To export the simulation results to an Instance Specification

- After running the simulation, do one of the following :
  - In the Variables pane of the Simulation window, click [ATTACHMENT filename='export_to_instance.png'] .
  - In the Variables pane of the Simulation window, right-click the simulated element and select Export Value To > Instance .
  - Specify the resultLocation tag in a Simulation Configuration . The instances with recorded values in slots are created at the end of the simulation.

The variables are evaluated and colored in red and/or green depending on whether there are any constraint failures in the Instance Table.

Verification Status

Display Legends

Verification Status

Display

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The instance table with highlighted passed and failed values.

The verification status and instances for constraints are recorded using the **VerificationStatus**stereotype that is applied to the **Value** specification of the properties you are working on. You can also see the details of the constraint failure in the tooltip when hovering the mouse over any highlighted values in the **Variables** tab of the Simulation window as shown in the following figure.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Variables pane displaying values with verification status.

In addition, the failed verdict values of [State Invariant](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/(2026x) State+invariant) are highlighted in red, and the passed values are highlighted in green. However, you must create a Dependency relationship between the test case and a value property as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Failed State Invariant with highlighted red values and the tooltip.

If the **VerificationStatus** state on the **Value** specification fails, the active validation will be marked for notification with the **fail** status tag as shown in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window showing verification status with the fail status tag.

The **VerificationStatus** stereotype, as shown in the figure above, has the tags as follows

| Tag | Description |
| --- | --- |
| constraint | The name of the failure constraint. |
| margin | The Requirement margin value calculated from value properties and the Requirement boundary (a satisfy Relation). |
| requirement | The name of the failure Requirement. |
| status | The result of the verification, either pass or fail. |
| timestamp | The timestamp of the first failure recorded. |

##### Remember Failure Status option

During the evaluation of particular configurations and scenarios, some Requirements/constraints can fail, and those values are marked in red. However, they may pass later and are marked in green again, but the failure is not recorded by default. To solve this problem, Simulation Toolkit provides the **Remember Failure Status** option in «SimulationConfig» to remember any failure status until the termination and record it as fail, even though it passes at the end. If the **Remember Failure Status** option is set true, and there are constraint failures during the simulation, the first fail status will be recorded in the status of «VerificationStatus» of new/recorded instance, and the time of the first failure will also be recorded in the **timestamp** tag as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the Remember Failure Status tag of a SimulationConfig to record the fail status and timestamp if available.

46423819

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=255626250 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00424: (2026x) References to elements with HTML

- page_id: `255626250`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/255626250/2026x+References+to+elements+with+HTML
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T18:43:35.669+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit > (2026x) Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Action languages
- labels: ['references-to-elements-using-html']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1247190977

1247190979

1247190978

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can make references to model Elements using HTML through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog_old' space='MT']. Those objects that allow references are ValueSpecification.Value, LiteralString.Value, OpaqueExpression.Body, OpaqueAction.Body, OpaqueBehavior.Body, Constraint.Specification, and ObjectFlow.Guard.

This feature has been developed to have references in text to model elements in various scripts. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit converts HTML into plain texts before execution. In the following figure, ValueSpecification.Value (b) can refer to slot **(b = 4)** of an InstanceSpecification (Ins). If the value of slot **(b = 4)** changes, ValueSpecification.Value (b) will change accordingly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A sample of references to elements with HTML.

1247190975

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Edit Hyperlink dialog_old' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f57f843-a0dd-4e41-a111-9f137d745d45"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1247190977</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d664f464-f526-4c03-9ca2-57b596b64969"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1247190979</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8eee16f8-42fa-4f9b-94b9-f980e2df467d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1247190978</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="inline-comment-marker">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can make references to model Elements using HTML through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog_old" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit Hyperlink dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Those objects that allow references are ValueSpecification.Value, LiteralString.Value, OpaqueExpression.Body, OpaqueAction.Body, OpaqueBehavior.Body, Constraint.Specification, and ObjectFlow.Guard.</p><p>This feature has been developed to have references in text to model elements in various scripts. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit converts HTML into plain texts before execution. In the following figure, ValueSpecification.Value (b) can refer to slot <strong>(b = 4)</strong> of an InstanceSpecification (Ins). If the value of slot <strong>(b = 4)</strong> changes, ValueSpecification.Value (b) will change accordingly.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="References to HTML elements.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) References to elements with HTML" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample of references to elements with HTML.</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d02e0bb1-f165-48ca-8df7-1d09022cbcbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1247190975</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog_old" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Edit Hyperlink dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
